## Overview

**wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery** is a personal portfolio site for Wayne Barrass-Brown — a static HTML gallery of artwork: drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, sketches, and similar work. The README describes it as *“A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc.”*

There is no build step, framework, or backend. Open `index.html` in a browser (or host the folder on GitHub Pages or similar) and it works.

---

## Project structure

| File / folder | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `index.html` | Main gallery page |
| `page2.html` | Alternate layout (“Responsive Page”) |
| `images/` | ~120+ artwork image files (JPG, PNG, JPEG) |
| `README.md` | Short project description |
| `LICENSE` | MIT License (2023) |

Git history is mostly incremental commits like “added art” as images were added.

---

## Two layout experiments

The site has two pages that show largely the same artwork in different layouts.

### 1. `index.html` — vertical column gallery

- Fixed **200px-wide** thumbnails in a **flexbox column** that fills the viewport height (`98vh`).
- A floating **header** on the left with the title “Wayne Barrass-Brown — Things I made and doodled”.
- Link to `page2.html` labeled “Responsive Page”.
- Inline CSS only; Poppins font is referenced but not loaded via a `<link>`.

### 2. `page2.html` — CSS Grid mosaic

- Inspired by a [Jen Simmons CodePen demo](https://codepen.io/jensimmons/pen/dybjNLQ) and MDN baseline docs (noted in comments).
- Uses **CSS Grid** with `grid-auto-flow: dense` and per-item `grid-row` / `grid-column` spans for a mosaic-style layout.
- Centered main area (~70% width), dark background, clipped edges.
- Includes a legacy `@supports (display: grid)` warning for very old browsers.

Both pages are long lists of hardcoded `<li><img src="images/...">` entries — no JavaScript, no templating, no lightbox.

---

## Content themes

From filenames, the collection includes:

- **Digital art** (iPad / Procreate): Iron Man, manga, eyes, hands, fantasy creatures
- **Traditional media**: pencil, acrylic, biro, scanned sketches
- **Pumpkin carving**: Ghostbusters, Disney, spiders
- **Figure studies**: torsos, poses, anatomy practice
- **Pop culture**: Captain America, manga/anime, Pokémon-style work

Some images appear more than once across the two pages (e.g. duplicates in the HTML).

---

## Technical notes

- **Static only** — no `package.json`, no bundler, no CMS.
- **Accessibility gaps** — most images have empty `alt=""` attributes.
- **Responsive design** — viewport meta is commented out on both pages; `page2.html` is more layout-focused but not fully mobile-oriented.
- **Maintenance** — adding art means editing HTML and dropping files into `images/`.

---

## How to view it

Serve or open the folder locally:

```powershell
cd d:\git\wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
start index.html
```

Or use any static file server (e.g. `python -m http.server`) if you want to avoid `file://` quirks.

In short: a lightweight, hand-built personal art portfolio — two HTML/CSS layout variants over a large folder of artwork images.

Yes — there’s a lot you can improve, and you don’t need a full rewrite. Here’s a practical list, ordered by impact vs effort.

## Quick wins (do these first)

**1. Fix the broken HTML in `index.html`**

Line 81 has mismatched tags:

```81:81:d:\git\wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery\index.html
            <h3></h3><a href="page2.html">Responsive Page</a></h3>
```

Should be something like `<p><a href="page2.html">Grid layout</a></p>`.

**2. Enable mobile viewing**

The viewport meta tag is commented out on both pages. Uncomment it:

```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
```

Without this, phones render the page zoomed out like a desktop site.

**3. Add meaningful `alt` text**

All ~100 images use `alt=""`. For a personal gallery, filenames are a fine starting point — e.g. `alt="Niffler pencil drawing"`. That helps screen readers and search engines.

**4. Remove duplicate entries**

Several pieces appear more than once on the same page, for example:
- `Hamster_0001.png` (3× on `index.html`)
- `Captain America Shield Acrylic Painting.jpg` (2×)
- `Scanned-image-22-0.jpg` and `20150801_083508.jpg` (2× each)

Deduplicating makes the gallery feel more intentional.

**5. Load the font you reference**

You set `font-family: 'Poppins'` but never load it. Add:

```html
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
```

Or drop Poppins and use a system font stack.

---

## UX improvements (high value for visitors)

**6. Click to view full size**

Right now everything is a small thumbnail. Wrapping images in links to the full file is a one-line change:

```html
<li><a href="images/Niffler pencil drawing.jpeg"><img src="..." alt="Niffler pencil drawing" loading="lazy"></a></li>
```

A lightbox (CSS-only or a tiny script) would be nicer still.

**7. Pick one layout (or make both actually responsive)**

You have two pages with almost the same images:
- `index.html` — vertical strip of 200px thumbnails
- `page2.html` — CSS Grid mosaic

`page2.html` only customizes the first 20 items with `nth-child` rules; the rest fall back to default grid cells. Either extend those rules, use a simpler auto-fit grid, or consolidate to one page with a layout toggle.

**8. Lazy-load images**

Add `loading="lazy"` to images below the fold. With 100+ full-resolution files, initial load will improve noticeably.

---

## Performance

**9. Optimize image files**

Many filenames suggest phone photos or scans (`DSC_*.jpg`, `IMG_*.jpg`). For web:
- Resize thumbnails to ~400–800px wide
- Compress JPEGs (often 60–80% smaller with little visible loss)
- Prefer WebP/AVIF with JPEG fallbacks if you want to go further

You can keep full-size originals for the “click to enlarge” view.

**10. Use `srcset` or separate thumbnails**

Serving a 4000px image in a 200px slot wastes bandwidth. A simple pattern:

```html
<img src="images/thumbs/hamster.jpg"
     srcset="images/hamster.jpg 2000w"
     sizes="200px"
     alt="Hamster drawing">
```

Even a build script that generates `images/thumbs/` would help a lot.

---

## Maintenance (save future-you time)

**11. Stop duplicating the image list**

The same ~100 `<li>` blocks exist in both HTML files. When you add art, you edit twice. Better options:

| Approach | Effort | Benefit |
|---|---|---|
| Single `images.json` + small JS to render the gallery | Low | Add art in one place |
| Tiny Python/Node script that reads `images/` and regenerates HTML | Low | No runtime JS needed |
| Static site generator (11ty, Astro) | Medium | Scales if the site grows |

**12. Clean up the repo**

- Add a `.gitignore` for `.DS_Store`, `*.psd`, and maybe `new images/`
- Remove committed `.DS_Store` files
- Rename vague files (`file.png`, `file3.png`) or drop them if unused
- Consider URL-safe filenames (hyphens instead of spaces) — spaces work but are awkward in URLs

**13. Fill in basic metadata**

Uncomment and use:
- `<meta name="description" content="...">`
- A favicon
- Open Graph tags if you share the site on social media

---

## Polish (optional but nice)

**14. Add light categorization**

Your filenames already suggest themes: Procreate/digital, pumpkin carving, figure studies, manga, pencil sketches. Even simple filter buttons (“Digital”, “Pumpkins”, “Sketches”) would make browsing easier than one long scroll.

**15. Deploy with GitHub Pages**

The site is already static — enable GitHub Pages on this repo and you get a live URL with almost no setup.

**16. Improve `README.md`**

A sentence on how to view locally, how to add new art, and the live URL (once deployed) would make the project clearer to others and to future you.

---

## Suggested priority

If I were tackling this in an afternoon:

1. Fix HTML + viewport + alt text  
2. Deduplicate images + add lazy loading + click-to-enlarge  
3. Merge to one page (or one shared image list)  
4. Add `.gitignore`, remove `.DS_Store`  
5. Compress images / generate thumbnails  
6. Deploy to GitHub Pages  

The site already works as a personal portfolio — these changes mostly improve mobile use, load time, accessibility, and how painful it is to add new work.

If you want, I can implement a specific slice — for example “single responsive page with lazy loading and lightbox” — and keep it static with no framework.

Added meaningful `alt` text to every image in `index.html` (119 images) and `page2.html` (121 images). None are left with empty `alt=""`.

The text is derived from each filename, with light cleanup:

- **Descriptive names** kept as-is: `"Niffler pencil drawing"`, `"Ironman Work In Progress 3"`
- **Number suffixes** stripped: `Hamster_0001` → `"Hamster"`, `Manga Girls_0003` → `"Manga Girls"`
- **Underscores** turned into spaces: `pumpkin_carving___ghostbusters` → `"pumpkin carving ghostbusters"`
- **DeviantArt filenames** shortened: `..._by_ulysses74_...-fullview` removed
- **Camera/phone filenames** given generic labels: `IMG_5216` → `"Artwork photograph 5216"`, `20150801_083226` → `"Artwork photograph"`
- **Vague filenames**: `file.png` → `"Artwork sketch"`, `RedLips1` → `"Red lips study"`

Do 14. Add light categorization

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Reviewing the gallery structure and image filenames to design category filters.

[REDACTED]

Building category filters from filename patterns and updating `index.html`.

[REDACTED]

Added light categorization to the gallery.

**Filter buttons** below the header:
All · Digital · Manga · Pumpkins · Sketches · Figures · Painting · Photos · Other

**How it works**
- Each gallery item has a `data-categories` attribute derived from its filename and alt text
- Items can belong to **multiple** categories (e.g. iPad fist study → `digital figures`)
- Clicking a filter shows matching items; **All** shows everything
- Active filter is highlighted (dark background)

**Approximate counts**
| Category | Items |
|---|---|
| Figures | 32 |
| Photos | 21 |
| Manga | 21 |
| Sketches | 20 |
| Other | 15 |
| Digital | 8 |
| Pumpkins | 8 |
| Painting | 3 |

Categories are filename-based, so a few pieces may land in an unexpected bucket (e.g. "Lampheads" matches "head" → Figures). You can tweak any item’s `data-categories` on its `<li>` tag if you want to move it.

Reload `index.html` and try the filter buttons.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

Add functionality so that I can right click on an image and select the category

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Adding a right-click context menu to assign categories, with choices saved in localStorage.

[REDACTED]

Adding a right-click context menu with checkboxes for multi-category selection, persisted in localStorage.

[REDACTED]

Right-click category selection is in place. Here's how it works:

**Usage**
- **Right-click** any gallery image
- A menu appears with all 8 categories as **checkboxes**
- Check or uncheck categories (an image can have several)
- Changes apply immediately and the active filter updates

**Persistence**
- Choices are saved in **localStorage** (key: `gallery-category-overrides`), keyed by image path
- They survive page reloads in the same browser
- They are not written into `index.html` — they're local to your browser

**Details**
- If you uncheck everything, the image falls back to **Other**
- Click outside the menu or press **Escape** to close it
- The normal browser right-click menu is suppressed on gallery images

Try right-clicking something miscategorized (e.g. "Lampheads" under Figures) and moving it to **Other**. If you want category edits saved permanently into the HTML file, that can be added as a separate export step.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

+            padding: 0.35rem 0.75rem;
+            font-size: 0.9rem;
+            cursor: pointer;

+
+        .category-menu label:hover {
+            background: #f2f2f2;
+        }
+    </style>

-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Generate the web-optimized display version:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+4. Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click an image in the gallery and change its categories in the browser. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

Add functionality for adding more images

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Adding an in-browser upload flow with IndexedDB persistence and an export path for committing new images to the repo.

[REDACTED]

Implementing an add-artwork panel with IndexedDB storage and export for publishing to git.

[REDACTED]

Added in-browser artwork upload with export for publishing.

**Add artwork (in the gallery)**
- **Add artwork** button opens a panel below the header
- Pick one or more images, set alt text and categories per preview
- **Add to gallery** saves them in IndexedDB and shows them at the top with a dashed outline
- **Export added artwork** downloads each image plus `gallery-additions.txt` with HTML snippets and steps to publish

**Publishing exported pieces**
1. Put downloaded images in `images/`
2. Paste the HTML from `gallery-additions.txt` into `<ul id="gallery">` in `index.html`
3. Run `python optimize_images.py`
4. Commit and push

**Other details**
- Right-click category edits work on uploaded images too
- Uploaded images persist in the browser across reloads until you export and add them to the repo
- `README.md` documents the browser and manual workflows

Reload `index.html`, click **Add artwork**, and try adding a test image. If you want, I can add a “Remove uploaded image” option for pieces you add by mistake before exporting.

--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,2 +1,72 @@
-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

+
+        .add-artwork {
+            margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
+            padding: 1rem;
+            border: 1px solid #2c2c2c;
+            background: #fafafa;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork h2 {
+            margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
+            font-size: 1.1rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__hint {
+            margin: 0 0 1rem;
+            font-size: 0.85rem;
+            color: #555;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__preview {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-direction: column;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            margin: 1rem 0;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__item {
+            display: grid;
+            grid-template-columns: 120px 1fr;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            padding: 0.75rem;
+            border: 1px solid #ddd;
+            background: #fff;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__thumb {
+            width: 100%;
+            height: auto;
+            display: block;
+            border: 1px solid #ccc;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields label {
+            display: block;
+            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
+            font-size: 0.85rem;
+        }
+

+            font-family: inherit;

+

+

+

+            gap: 0.5rem;
+        }

+        }

+        <div class="gallery-actions">
+            <button type="button" id="toggle-add-artwork">Add artwork</button>
+            <button type="button" id="export-uploads" hidden>Export added artwork</button>
+        </div>
+        <section id="add-artwork" class="add-artwork" hidden>
+            <h2>Add artwork</h2>
+            <p class="add-artwork__hint">
+                Choose images to preview them here, set alt text and categories, then add them to the gallery.
+                New pieces are saved in this browser until you export them into the project folder.
+            </p>
+            <input type="file" id="add-artwork-files" accept="image/*" multiple>
+            <div id="add-artwork-preview" class="add-artwork__preview"></div>
+            <div class="add-artwork__actions">
+                <button type="button" id="add-artwork-submit" disabled>Add to gallery</button>
+                <button type="button" id="add-artwork-clear" disabled>Clear selection</button>
+                <button type="button" id="add-artwork-close">Close</button>
+            </div>
+        </section>
+        <nav class="filters" aria-label="Filter artwork by category">
+            <button type="button" data-filter="all" aria-pressed="true">All</button>
+            <button type="button" data-filter="digital" aria-pressed="false">Digital</button>
+            <button type="button" data-filter="manga" aria-pressed="false">Manga</button>
+            <button type="button" data-filter="pumpkins" aria-pressed="false">Pumpkins</button>
+            <button type="button" data-filter="sketches" aria-pressed="false">Sketches</button>

+            <button type="button" data-filter="painting" aria-pressed="false">Painting</button>
+            <button type="button" data-filter="photos" aria-pressed="false">Photos</button>

The page is style is a bit boring. What can you do to make it more engaging, vibrant and so that it works well on all devices?

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Reviewing the current styles, then applying a more vibrant, responsive design.

[REDACTED]

Applying a responsive visual refresh: warmer palette, card-based gallery grid, improved typography, and mobile-friendly interactions.

[REDACTED]

Wrapping the filter bar in a sticky toolbar container.

[REDACTED]

Gave the gallery a visual refresh aimed at feeling more vibrant and working better across screen sizes. Here’s what changed:

## Visual design
- **Warm gradient background** with soft coral and purple glows
- **Gradient hero header** with clearer hierarchy: eyebrow label, name, tagline
- **Purple/coral accent palette** via CSS variables for consistent styling
- **Card-style artwork tiles** with rounded corners, shadows, and hover lift/zoom
- **Pill-shaped buttons** with gradients on primary actions and active filters
- **Subtle fade-in** when gallery items load

## Responsive layout
- **Fluid typography and spacing** using `clamp()` so type scales on phones and desktops
- **Responsive grid gallery**: 1 column on narrow screens, multiple columns as space allows (`auto-fill`, min 260px)
- **Wider canvas** on large screens (up to 1120px instead of 720px)
- **Sticky filter bar** with frosted glass effect — stays visible while scrolling
- **Horizontal scroll filters on mobile** so category buttons don’t wrap awkwardly on small screens
- **Add-artwork previews stack** on phones (thumbnail above fields)

## Accessibility & polish
- **Larger touch targets** (min 44px button height)
- **Visible focus rings** for keyboard users
- **`prefers-reduced-motion`** respected — animations disabled when requested
- **Theme color** set for mobile browser chrome

Reload the page to see the update. If you want to push it further, options include a dark mode toggle, category-colored filter chips, or a lightbox instead of opening images in a new tab.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,2 +1,72 @@
-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

I love it although I'm looking for something more professional. Maybe a little bit of corporate crossover. Also there is quite a bit of white space between images of different sizes, so can we address this and make them fill vertically. Not sure what the best approach would be but some of the images are very, very wide so maybe make them span the full width of the section.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Refining the design toward a more professional corporate look and implementing a masonry layout where wide images span the full section width.

-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

     <style>
+        :root {
+            --bg-top: #fff7f0;
+            --bg-bottom: #efe8ff;
+            --surface: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
+            --surface-strong: #ffffff;
+            --text: #1f1833;
+            --text-muted: #5c5470;
+            --accent: #5b2cff;
+            --accent-soft: #efe8ff;
+            --accent-warm: #ff6b4a;
+            --shadow: 0 14px 40px rgba(48, 32, 96, 0.12);
+            --shadow-hover: 0 20px 50px rgba(48, 32, 96, 0.18);
+            --radius-lg: 1.25rem;
+            --radius-md: 0.9rem;
+            --radius-pill: 999px;
+        }
+
         * {
             box-sizing: border-box;
         }
 
         html {
-            font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif;
-            font-size: 18px;
+            font-family: "Poppins", sans-serif;
+            font-size: clamp(16px, 1.5vw, 18px);
+            color: var(--text);
+            scroll-behavior: smooth;
         }
 
         body {
             margin: 0;
+            min-height: 100vh;
+            background:
+                radial-gradient(circle at top left, rgba(255, 107, 74, 0.18), transparent 28rem),
+                radial-gradient(circle at top right, rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.16), transparent 30rem),
+                linear-gradient(180deg, var(--bg-top) 0%, var(--bg-bottom) 100%);
         }
 
         main {
-            max-width: 720px;
+            width: min(1120px, 100%);
             margin: 0 auto;
-            padding: 1rem;
+            padding: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 2rem);
+        }
+
+        .site-header {
+            margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
+            padding: clamp(1.25rem, 4vw, 2rem);
+            border-radius: calc(var(--radius-lg) + 0.25rem);
+            color: #fff;
+            background:
+                linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12), transparent 55%),
+                linear-gradient(135deg, #5b2cff 0%, #7b3fff 38%, #ff6b4a 100%);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+        }
+
+        .site-header__eyebrow {
+            margin: 0 0 0.35rem;
+            font-size: 0.8rem;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            letter-spacing: 0.14em;
+            text-transform: uppercase;
+            opacity: 0.9;
         }
 
-        header {
-            margin-bottom: 2rem;
+        .site-header h1 {
+            margin: 0;
+            font-size: clamp(2rem, 6vw, 3.2rem);
+            line-height: 1.05;
+            font-weight: 700;
+        }
+
+        .site-header__tagline {
+            margin: 0.75rem 0 0;
+            max-width: 34rem;
+            font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 1.2rem);
+            line-height: 1.5;
+            color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.92);
+        }
+
+        .gallery-actions,
+        .add-artwork__actions {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.65rem;
         }
 
-        h1 {
-            font-size: 2rem;
+        .gallery-actions {
+            margin-bottom: 1rem;
+        }
+
+        button {
+            font-family: inherit;
+            font-size: 0.92rem;
+            font-weight: 500;
+            min-height: 2.75rem;
+            padding: 0.55rem 1rem;
+            border: 1px solid transparent;
+            border-radius: var(--radius-pill);
+            background: var(--surface-strong);
+            color: var(--text);
+            box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(48, 32, 96, 0.08);
+            cursor: pointer;
+            transition: transform 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease, background 0.18s ease, color 0.18s ease;
+        }
+
+        button:hover {
+            transform: translateY(-1px);
+            box-shadow: 0 12px 28px rgba(48, 32, 96, 0.12);
+        }
+
+        button:focus-visible {
+            outline: 3px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.35);
+            outline-offset: 2px;
+        }
+
+        #toggle-add-artwork {
+            background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent) 0%, #7b3fff 100%);
             color: #fff;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork {
+            margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
+            padding: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 1.25rem);
+            border: 1px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.12);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork h2 {
+            margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
+            font-size: 1.15rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__hint {
+            margin: 0 0 1rem;
+            font-size: 0.92rem;
+            line-height: 1.5;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__preview {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-direction: column;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            margin: 1rem 0;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__item {
+            display: grid;
+            grid-template-columns: 120px 1fr;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            padding: 0.9rem;
+            border: 1px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.1);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface-strong);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__thumb {
+            width: 100%;
+            height: auto;
+            display: block;
+            border-radius: 0.65rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields label {
+            display: block;
+            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
+            font-size: 0.85rem;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields input[type="text"] {
+            width: 100%;
+            font-family: inherit;
+            font-size: 0.95rem;
+            padding: 0.55rem 0.7rem;
+            margin-top: 0.25rem;
+            border: 1px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.15);
+            border-radius: 0.65rem;
+            background: #fff;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__categories {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.5rem 1rem;
+            margin-top: 0.5rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__categories label {
+            display: inline-flex;
+            align-items: center;
+            gap: 0.35rem;
             margin: 0;
-            padding: 0.75em 0.25em;
-            background: #2c2c2c;
-            line-height: 1.1em;
+            color: var(--text);
+        }
+
+        .toolbar {
+            position: sticky;
+            top: 0.75rem;
+            z-index: 20;
+            margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
+            padding: 0.85rem;
+            border: 1px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.1);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78);
+            backdrop-filter: blur(14px);
+            box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(48, 32, 96, 0.08);
         }
 
-        ul {

+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.55rem;
+        }
+

+            background: var(--accent-soft);

+            box-shadow: none;
+        }
+
+        .filters button[aria-pressed="true"] {
+            background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent) 0%, #7b3fff 100%);

+            box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.24);
+        }
+
+        #gallery {
             padding: 0;
             margin: 0;
-            display: flex;
-            flex-direction: column;
-            gap: 1.5rem;
+            display: grid;
+            grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(min(100%, 260px), 1fr));
+            gap: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 1.5rem);
         }
 
-        li {
-            list-style-type: none;
+        #gallery li {
+            list-style: none;
+            animation: fade-up 0.45s ease both;
         }
 
-        li a {

             display: block;
+            overflow: hidden;
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface-strong);

+            transition: transform 0.22s ease, box-shadow 0.22s ease;
         }
 
-        img {
+        #gallery li a:hover {
+            transform: translateY(-4px);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow-hover);
+        }
+
+        #gallery img {
             width: 100%;
             height: auto;
             display: block;
-            border: 1px solid black;
-            box-shadow: 1px 3px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
+            border: 0;
+            box-shadow: none;

         }
-    </style>
 
-    <script>
+        #gallery li a:hover img {

-    </script>
+        #gallery li.is-hidden {
+            display: none;

+

+            outline: 2px dashed var(--accent-warm);
+            outline-offset: 3px;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu {
+            position: fixed;
+            z-index: 1000;
+            min-width: 11rem;
+            padding: 0.5rem 0;
+            border: 1px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.12);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface-strong);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow-hover);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu__title {
+            margin: 0;
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem 0.55rem;
+            font-size: 0.8rem;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+            border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(91, 44, 255, 0.1);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu label {
+            display: flex;
+            align-items: center;
+            gap: 0.5rem;
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem;
+            font-size: 0.9rem;
+            cursor: pointer;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu label:hover {
+            background: var(--accent-soft);
+        }
+
+        @keyframes fade-up {
+            from {
+                opacity: 0;
+                transform: translateY(12px);
+            }
+
+            to {
+                opacity: 1;
+                transform: translateY(0);
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (max-width: 640px) {
+            .toolbar {
+                top: 0.5rem;
+                padding: 0.75rem;
+            }
+
+            .filters {
+                flex-wrap: nowrap;
+                overflow-x: auto;
+                padding-bottom: 0.15rem;
+                scrollbar-width: thin;
+            }
+
+            .filters button {
+                flex: 0 0 auto;
+            }
+
+            .add-artwork__item {
+                grid-template-columns: 1fr;
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+            html {
+                scroll-behavior: auto;
+            }
+
+            *,
+            *::before,
+            *::after {
+                animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+                animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
+                transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+            }
+        }
+    </style>
 </head>

I love it although I'm looking for something more professional. Maybe a little bit of corporate crossover. Also there is quite a bit of white space between images of different sizes, so can we address this and make them fill vertically. Not sure what the best approach would be but some of the images are very, very wide so maybe make them span the full width of the section.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Updating the design to a cleaner corporate style and switching the gallery to a masonry column layout with full-width spans for wide images.

[REDACTED]

Adding JavaScript to detect wide images and apply the masonry layout.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what changed:

### More professional look
- Swapped the purple/coral gradient theme for a **navy and slate** palette with corporate blue accents (`#162033`, `#0f4c81`)
- Flat light-gray background instead of colorful gradients
- Cleaner header, buttons, and filter bar — subtle borders and shadows instead of lift/scale hover effects
- Tighter typography and spacing for a more portfolio/corporate feel

### Better image layout (less whitespace)
- Replaced the equal-height card grid with a **masonry column layout** (`column-count: 3` on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile)
- Images pack vertically by natural height, so mixed aspect ratios no longer leave large gaps between rows

### Full-width wide images
- On load, each image is measured; anything with aspect ratio **≥ 1.55:1** (wider than ~3:2) gets the `gallery-item--wide` class and **spans the full gallery width** via `column-span: all`
- Panoramas and montages should now stretch edge-to-edge while portraits and squares stay in the columns

Refresh `index.html` locally to see it. If too many or too few images go full-width, we can adjust the `WIDE_ASPECT_RATIO` threshold (currently `1.55`).

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

--- a/README.md

@@ -1,2 +1,72 @@
-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

         * {
             box-sizing: border-box;

-            font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif;

             margin: 0;
+            min-height: 100vh;
+            background: var(--bg);
         }
 
         main {
-            max-width: 720px;
+            width: min(1180px, 100%);
             margin: 0 auto;
-            padding: 1rem;
+            padding: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 2rem);
+        }
+
+        .site-header {
+            margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
+            padding: clamp(1.5rem, 4vw, 2.25rem);
+            border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            color: #fff;
+            background: linear-gradient(180deg, #1b2a41 0%, var(--navy) 100%);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+            border-bottom: 3px solid var(--accent);
+        }
+
+        .site-header__eyebrow {
+            margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
+            font-size: 0.75rem;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            letter-spacing: 0.16em;
+            text-transform: uppercase;
+            color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.72);
         }
 
-        header {
-            margin-bottom: 2rem;
+        .site-header h1 {
+            margin: 0;
+            font-size: clamp(1.85rem, 5vw, 2.75rem);
+            line-height: 1.1;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
+        }
+
+        .site-header__tagline {
+            margin: 0.65rem 0 0;
+            max-width: 36rem;
+            font-size: clamp(0.95rem, 2.2vw, 1.05rem);
+            line-height: 1.55;
+            color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78);
         }
 
-        h1 {
-            font-size: 2rem;
+        .gallery-actions,
+        .add-artwork__actions {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.65rem;
+        }
+
+        .gallery-actions {
+            margin-bottom: 1rem;
+        }
+
+        button {
+            font-family: inherit;
+            font-size: 0.88rem;
+            font-weight: 500;
+            min-height: 2.6rem;
+            padding: 0.5rem 0.95rem;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-pill);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            color: var(--text);
+            cursor: pointer;
+            transition: background 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, color 0.18s ease;
+        }
+
+        button:hover {
+            border-color: #b8c3d1;
+            background: #f8fafc;
+        }
+
+        button:focus-visible {
+            outline: 3px solid rgba(15, 76, 129, 0.28);
+            outline-offset: 2px;
+        }
+
+        #toggle-add-artwork {
+            background: var(--accent);
+            border-color: var(--accent);
             color: #fff;
+        }
+
+        #toggle-add-artwork:hover {
+            background: #0c3f6b;
+            border-color: #0c3f6b;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork {
+            margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
+            padding: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 1.25rem);
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork h2 {
+            margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
+            font-size: 1.15rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__hint {
+            margin: 0 0 1rem;
+            font-size: 0.92rem;
+            line-height: 1.5;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__preview {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-direction: column;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            margin: 1rem 0;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__item {
+            display: grid;
+            grid-template-columns: 120px 1fr;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            padding: 0.9rem;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__thumb {
+            width: 100%;
+            height: auto;
+            display: block;
+            border-radius: 0.65rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields label {
+            display: block;
+            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
+            font-size: 0.85rem;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields input[type="text"] {
+            width: 100%;
+            font-family: inherit;
+            font-size: 0.95rem;
+            padding: 0.55rem 0.7rem;
+            margin-top: 0.25rem;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: #fff;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__categories {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.5rem 1rem;
+            margin-top: 0.5rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__categories label {
+            display: inline-flex;
+            align-items: center;
+            gap: 0.35rem;
             margin: 0;
-            padding: 0.75em 0.25em;
-            background: #2c2c2c;
-            line-height: 1.1em;
+            color: var(--text);
         }
 
-        ul {
+        .toolbar {
+            position: sticky;
+            top: 0.75rem;
+            z-index: 20;
+            margin-bottom: 1rem;
+            padding: 0.75rem;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.94);
+            backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+        }
+
+        .filters {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.55rem;
+        }
+
+        .filters button {
+            background: #fff;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .filters button[aria-pressed="true"] {
+            background: var(--accent);
+            border-color: var(--accent);
+            color: #fff;
+        }
+
+        #gallery {
             padding: 0;
             margin: 0;
-            display: flex;
-            flex-direction: column;
-            gap: 1.5rem;
+            column-gap: var(--gallery-gap);
+            column-count: 3;
+        }
+
+        #gallery li {
+            list-style: none;
+            display: inline-block;
+            width: 100%;
+            margin: 0 0 var(--gallery-gap);
+            break-inside: avoid;
+            animation: fade-up 0.35s ease both;
         }
 
-        li {
-            list-style-type: none;
+        #gallery li.gallery-item--wide {
+            column-span: all;
         }
 
-        li a {
+        #gallery li a {
             display: block;
+            overflow: hidden;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+            transition: border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease;
         }
 
-        img {
+        #gallery li a:hover {
+            border-color: #b8c3d1;
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow-hover);
+        }
+
+        #gallery img {
             width: 100%;
             height: auto;
             display: block;
-            border: 1px solid black;
-            box-shadow: 1px 3px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
+            border: 0;
         }
-    </style>
 
-    <script>
+        #gallery li.is-hidden {
+            display: none;
+        }
 
-    </script>
+        #gallery li[data-uploaded="true"] a {
+            outline: 2px dashed var(--accent);
+            outline-offset: 2px;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu {
+            position: fixed;
+            z-index: 1000;
+            min-width: 11rem;
+            padding: 0.5rem 0;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow-hover);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu__title {
+            margin: 0;
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem 0.55rem;
+            font-size: 0.8rem;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu label {
+            display: flex;
+            align-items: center;
+            gap: 0.5rem;
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem;
+            font-size: 0.9rem;
+            cursor: pointer;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu label:hover {
+            background: var(--accent-soft);
+        }
+
+        @keyframes fade-up {
+            from {
+                opacity: 0;
+                transform: translateY(12px);
+            }
+
+            to {
+                opacity: 1;
+                transform: translateY(0);
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (max-width: 900px) {
+            #gallery {
+                column-count: 2;
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (max-width: 640px) {
+            .toolbar {
+                top: 0.5rem;
+                padding: 0.75rem;
+            }
+
+            #gallery {
+                column-count: 1;
+            }
+
+            .filters {
+                flex-wrap: nowrap;
+                overflow-x: auto;
+                padding-bottom: 0.15rem;
+                scrollbar-width: thin;
+            }
+
+            .filters button {
+                flex: 0 0 auto;
+            }
+
+            .add-artwork__item {
+                grid-template-columns: 1fr;
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+            html {
+                scroll-behavior: auto;
+            }
+
+            *,
+            *::before,
+            *::after {
+                animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+                animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
+                transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+            }
+        }
+    </style>
 </head>

Add right click functionality for deleting an image

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Reviewing the existing right-click context menu so we can add delete support.

[REDACTED]

Adding a delete option to the existing right-click menu: remove browser-added artwork from IndexedDB, and hide built-in gallery images locally via localStorage.

[REDACTED]

Right-click any gallery image to open the context menu — there’s now a delete option at the bottom.

**How it works**

- **Added artwork** (dashed outline): choose **Remove added artwork** to delete it from the gallery and from browser storage (IndexedDB).
- **Built-in gallery images**: choose **Hide image** to remove it from view in this browser only. The choice is saved in localStorage and persists on reload. To remove it permanently, you still need to delete the entry from `index.html`.

Both actions ask for confirmation before proceeding. Category overrides are cleared when an image is hidden or removed.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

--- a/README.md

@@ -1,2 +1,72 @@
-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

+        }
+
+        .filters {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-wrap: wrap;
+            gap: 0.55rem;
+        }
+
+        .filters button {
+            background: #fff;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .filters button[aria-pressed="true"] {
+            background: var(--accent);
+            border-color: var(--accent);
+            color: #fff;
         }
 
-        ul {
+        #gallery {
             padding: 0;
             margin: 0;
-            display: flex;
-            flex-direction: column;
-            gap: 1.5rem;
+            column-gap: var(--gallery-gap);
+            column-count: 3;
         }
 
-        li {
-            list-style-type: none;
+        #gallery li {
+            list-style: none;
+            display: inline-block;
+            width: 100%;
+            margin: 0 0 var(--gallery-gap);
+            break-inside: avoid;
+            animation: fade-up 0.35s ease both;
+        }
+
+        #gallery li.gallery-item--wide {
+            column-span: all;
         }
 
-        li a {
+        #gallery li a {
             display: block;
+            overflow: hidden;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+            transition: border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease;
+        }
+
+        #gallery li a:hover {
+            border-color: #b8c3d1;
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow-hover);
         }
 
-        img {
+        #gallery img {
             width: 100%;
             height: auto;
             display: block;
-            border: 1px solid black;
-            box-shadow: 1px 3px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
+            border: 0;
         }
-    </style>
 
-    <script>
+        #gallery li.is-hidden {
+            display: none;
+        }
 
-    </script>
+        #gallery li[data-uploaded="true"] a {
+            outline: 2px dashed var(--accent);
+            outline-offset: 2px;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu {
+            position: fixed;
+            z-index: 1000;
+            min-width: 11rem;
+            padding: 0.5rem 0;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow-hover);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu__title {
+            margin: 0;
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem 0.55rem;
+            font-size: 0.8rem;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu label {
+            display: flex;
+            align-items: center;
+            gap: 0.5rem;
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem;
+            font-size: 0.9rem;
+            cursor: pointer;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu label:hover {
+            background: var(--accent-soft);
+        }
+
+        .category-menu__actions {
+            border-top: 1px solid var(--border);
+            padding: 0.45rem 0.5rem 0.5rem;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu__delete {
+            width: 100%;
+            min-height: 2.25rem;
+            font-size: 0.85rem;
+            color: #b42318;
+            border-color: #fecdca;
+            background: #fff;
+            box-shadow: none;
+        }
+
+        .category-menu__delete:hover {
+            background: #fef3f2;
+            border-color: #fda29b;
+        }
+
+        @keyframes fade-up {
+            from {
+                opacity: 0;
+                transform: translateY(12px);
+            }
+
+            to {
+                opacity: 1;
+                transform: translateY(0);
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (max-width: 900px) {
+            #gallery {
+                column-count: 2;
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (max-width: 640px) {
+            .toolbar {
+                top: 0.5rem;
+                padding: 0.75rem;
+            }
+
+            #gallery {
+                column-count: 1;
+            }
+
+            .filters {
+                flex-wrap: nowrap;
+                overflow-x: auto;
+                padding-bottom: 0.15rem;
+                scrollbar-width: thin;
+            }
+
+            .filters button {
+                flex: 0 0 auto;
+            }
+
+            .add-artwork__item {
+                grid-template-columns: 1fr;
+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+            html {
+                scroll-behavior: auto;
+            }
+
+            *,
+            *::before,
+            *::after {
+                animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+                animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
+                transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+            }
+        }
+    </style>
 </head>

+            <button type="button" id="toggle-add-artwork">Add artwork</button>

+        </div>
+        <section id="add-artwork" class="add-artwork" hidden>
+            <h2>Add artwork</h2>
+            <p class="add-artwork__hint">
+                Choose images to preview them here, set alt text and categories, then add them to the gallery.
+                New pieces are saved in this browser until you export them into the project folder.

--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,2 +1,72 @@
-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |

     <style>
+        :root {
+            --bg: #f3f5f8;
+            --surface: #ffffff;
+            --border: #d7dde5;
+            --text: #162033;
+            --text-muted: #5c6778;
+            --accent: #0f4c81;
+            --accent-soft: #e8f1fa;
+            --navy: #162033;
+            --shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(22, 32, 51, 0.06), 0 8px 24px rgba(22, 32, 51, 0.06);
+            --shadow-hover: 0 10px 28px rgba(22, 32, 51, 0.1);
+            --radius-lg: 1rem;
+            --radius-md: 0.7rem;
+            --radius-pill: 999px;
+            --gallery-gap: 0.85rem;
+        }
+
         * {
             box-sizing: border-box;
         }
 
         html {
-            font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif;
-            font-size: 18px;
+            font-family: "Poppins", sans-serif;

+            color: var(--text);
+            scroll-behavior: smooth;
         }
 
         body {
             margin: 0;
+            min-height: 100vh;
+            background: var(--bg);
         }
 
         main {
-            max-width: 720px;

             margin: 0 auto;

+

+            border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            color: #fff;
+            background: linear-gradient(180deg, #1b2a41 0%, var(--navy) 100%);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+            border-bottom: 3px solid var(--accent);
+        }
+
+        .site-header__eyebrow {
+            margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
+            font-size: 0.75rem;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            letter-spacing: 0.16em;
+            text-transform: uppercase;
+            color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.72);
+        }
+
+        .site-header h1 {
+            margin: 0;
+            font-size: clamp(1.85rem, 5vw, 2.75rem);
+            line-height: 1.1;
+            font-weight: 600;
+            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
+        }
+
+        .site-header__tagline {

+            max-width: 36rem;
+            font-size: clamp(0.95rem, 2.2vw, 1.05rem);
+            line-height: 1.55;
+            color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78);
         }
 
-        header {
-            margin-bottom: 2rem;

+        .add-artwork__actions {

+            gap: 0.65rem;

+
+        .gallery-actions {
+            margin-bottom: 1rem;
         }
 
-        h1 {
-            font-size: 2rem;
+        button {
+            font-family: inherit;
+            font-size: 0.88rem;
+            font-weight: 500;
+            min-height: 2.6rem;
+            padding: 0.5rem 0.95rem;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-pill);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            color: var(--text);
+            cursor: pointer;
+            transition: background 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, color 0.18s ease;
+        }
+
+        button:hover {
+            border-color: #b8c3d1;
+            background: #f8fafc;
+        }
+
+        button:focus-visible {
+            outline: 3px solid rgba(15, 76, 129, 0.28);
+            outline-offset: 2px;
+        }
+
+        #toggle-add-artwork {
+            background: var(--accent);
+            border-color: var(--accent);
             color: #fff;
+        }
+
+        #toggle-add-artwork:hover {
+            background: #0c3f6b;
+            border-color: #0c3f6b;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork {
+            margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
+            padding: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 1.25rem);
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            background: var(--surface);
+            box-shadow: var(--shadow);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork[hidden] {
+            display: none;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork h2 {
+            margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
+            font-size: 1.15rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__hint {
+            margin: 0 0 1rem;
+            font-size: 0.92rem;
+            line-height: 1.5;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__preview {
+            display: flex;
+            flex-direction: column;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            margin: 1rem 0;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__item {
+            display: grid;
+            grid-template-columns: 120px 1fr;
+            gap: 1rem;
+            padding: 0.9rem;
+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+            background: var(--surface);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__thumb {
+            width: 100%;
+            height: auto;
+            display: block;
+            border-radius: var(--radius-md);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields label {
+            display: block;
+            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
+            font-size: 0.85rem;
+            color: var(--text-muted);
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__fields input[type="text"] {

+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__categories {
+            display: flex;

+            gap: 0.5rem 1rem;
+            margin-top: 0.5rem;
+        }
+
+        .add-artwork__categories label {
+            display: inline-flex;
+            align-items: center;
+            gap: 0.35rem;
             margin: 0;
-            padding: 0.75em 0.25em;
-            background: #2c2c2c;

+        }
+
+        .toolbar {
+            position: sticky;
+            top: 0.75rem;
+            z-index: 20;
+            margin-bottom: 1rem;

+            border: 1px solid var(--border);
+            border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
+            background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.94);
+            backdrop-filter: blur(10px);

+
+        .filters {

+            gap: 0.55rem;
+        }
+
+        .filters button {
+            background: #fff;

+        }
+
+        .filters button[aria-pressed="true"] {
+            background: var(--accent);

+            color: #fff;
         }

+
+        @keyframes fade-up {

+
+        @media (max-width: 900px) {
+            #gallery {
+                column-count: 2;

+
+        @media (max-width: 640px) {

+                top: 0.5rem;

+            }
+
+            #gallery {
+                column-count: 1;

+

+            }
+
+            .add-artwork__item {

+            }
+        }
+
+        @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+            html {
+                scroll-behavior: auto;
+            }
+
+            *,
+            *::before,
+            *::after {
+                animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+                animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
+                transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
+            }
+        }

Where is the delete function? I only see Remove.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Checking how the right-click menu labels the delete action.

[REDACTED]

The menu relabels the button on open — uploaded items show "Remove added artwork" instead of "Delete". Updating it to always say "Delete image".

[REDACTED]

The delete action is in the **right-click context menu** on any gallery image — scroll to the bottom, below the category checkboxes. The button is red and labeled **Delete image**.

The JavaScript handler is `deleteGalleryItem()` in `index.html`, wired to the `#category-menu-delete` button.

Previously, opening the menu **replaced** that label with "Remove added artwork" (browser-added images) or "Hide image" (built-in gallery images), which is why you only saw "Remove". It now stays **Delete image** for both types; the confirmation dialog still explains the difference:

- **Added artwork** — deleted from the gallery and browser storage
- **Built-in images** — removed from view in this browser only (still in `index.html` until you edit the file)

Right-click an image and you should see **Delete image** at the bottom of the menu.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

--- a/README.md

@@ -1,2 +1,72 @@
-# wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery
-A collection of things I've made, drawn, painted, carved, modelled etc
+# Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+A personal portfolio site for drawings, paintings, digital art, pumpkin carvings, and other work. The site is a single static page (`index.html`) with category filters, lazy-loaded images, and click-through to full-resolution files.
+
+Repository: [github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery)
+
+## View locally
+
+Open `index.html` in your browser, or run a simple local server from the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+python -m http.server 8000
+```
+
+Then visit [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000).
+
+## Live site
+
+This project can be published with [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com/) by enabling Pages for the `main` branch in the repository settings. Once enabled, the site will be available at:
+
+`https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/`
+
+## Adding new artwork
+
+### In the browser
+
+1. Open the gallery and click **Add artwork**.
+2. Choose one or more image files.
+3. Set alt text and categories for each preview.
+4. Click **Add to gallery**.
+
+New pieces appear at the top of the gallery with a dashed outline. They are stored in this browser until you export them.
+
+5. Click **Export added artwork** to download:
+   - each image file
+   - a `gallery-additions.txt` file with HTML snippets and publish steps
+
+6. Copy the downloaded images into `images/`, paste the HTML into `index.html`, then run:
+
+```powershell
+pip install Pillow
+python optimize_images.py
+```
+
+### Manually
+
+1. Copy the image file into `images/`.
+2. Add a gallery entry to `index.html` inside `<ul id="gallery">`:
+
+```html
+<li data-categories="sketches">
+  <a href="images/my-new-piece.jpg">
+    <img src="images/display/my-new-piece.jpg" alt="My new piece" loading="lazy">
+  </a>
+</li>
+```
+
+3. Run `python optimize_images.py`.
+
+Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketches`, `figures`, `painting`, `photos`, `other`.
+
+You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
+
+## Project structure
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+| --- | --- |
+| `index.html` | Gallery page |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
+| `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `LICENSE` | MIT License |
