+        const categoryList = document.getElementById("category-list");
+        const addCategoryForm = document.getElementById("add-category-form");
+        const newCategoryLabelInput = document.getElementById("new-category-label");
 
+        let categories = [];
         let activeFilter = "all";
         let activeItem = null;
         let pendingUploads = [];
@@ -690,6 +849,285 @@
                 .trim();
         }
 
+        function escapeAttr(value) {
+            return String(value)
+                .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
+                .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
+                .replace(/</g, "&lt;");
+        }
+
+        function slugifyCategory(label) {
+            const slug = label.toLowerCase().trim()
+                .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-")
+                .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, "");
+            return slug || "other";
+        }
+
+        function loadCategories() {
+            try {
+                const stored = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(CATEGORIES_STORAGE_KEY) || "null");
+                if (Array.isArray(stored) && stored.length) {
+                    return stored;
+                }
+            } catch {
+                // fall through to defaults
+            }
+            return DEFAULT_CATEGORIES.map((category) => ({ ...category }));
+        }
+
+        function saveCategoriesList() {
+            localStorage.setItem(CATEGORIES_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(categories));
+        }
+
+        function getCategoryLabel(categoryId) {
+            return categories.find((category) => category.id === categoryId)?.label || categoryId;
+        }
+
+        function remapCategoryList(list, oldId, newId) {
+            if (!Array.isArray(list) || !list.length) {
+                return ["other"];
+            }
+            const remapped = list.map((category) => (category === oldId ? newId : category));
+            const unique = [...new Set(remapped)];
+            return unique.length ? unique : ["other"];
+        }
+
+        function removeCategoryFromList(list, categoryId) {
+            if (!Array.isArray(list) || !list.length) {
+                return ["other"];
+            }
+            const filtered = list.filter((category) => category !== categoryId);
+            return filtered.length ? filtered : ["other"];
+        }
+
+        function mergeDiscoveredCategories() {
+            const known = new Set(categories.map((category) => category.id));
+            const discovered = [];
+
+            const registerCategory = (categoryId) => {
+                if (!known.has(categoryId)) {
+                    discovered.push(categoryId);
+                    known.add(categoryId);
+                }
+            };
+
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                getCategories(item).forEach(registerCategory);
+            });
+            loadHiddenRecords().forEach((record) => {
+                record.categories?.forEach(registerCategory);
+            });
+
+            discovered.sort().forEach((categoryId) => {
+                categories.push({
+                    id: categoryId,
+                    label: categoryId
+                        .split("-")
+                        .map((part) => part.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + part.slice(1))
+                        .join(" "),
+                });
+            });
+
+            if (discovered.length) {
+                saveCategoriesList();
+            }
+        }
+
+        async function countCategoryUsage(categoryId) {
+            let count = 0;
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                if (getCategories(item).includes(categoryId)) {
+                    count += 1;
+                }
+            });
+            loadHiddenRecords().forEach((record) => {
+                if (record.categories?.includes(categoryId)) {
+                    count += 1;
+                }
+            });
+            const uploads = await readUploads();
+            uploads.forEach((upload) => {
+                if (upload.categories?.includes(categoryId)) {
+                    count += 1;
+                }
+            });
+            return count;
+        }
+
+        function migrateCategoryId(oldId, newId) {
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                const itemCategories = getCategories(item);
+                if (itemCategories.includes(oldId)) {
+                    setCategories(item, remapCategoryList(itemCategories, oldId, newId));
+                }
+            });
+
+            const overrides = loadOverrides();
+            Object.keys(overrides).forEach((key) => {
+                if (overrides[key].includes(oldId)) {
+                    overrides[key] = remapCategoryList(overrides[key], oldId, newId);
+                }
+            });
+            localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(overrides));
+
+            const hidden = loadHiddenRecords();
+            hidden.forEach((record) => {
+                record.categories = remapCategoryList(record.categories, oldId, newId);
+            });
+            saveHiddenRecords(hidden);
+        }
+
+        async function migrateCategoryIdInUploads(oldId, newId) {
+            const uploads = await readUploads();
+            const changedUploads = uploads.filter((upload) => upload.categories?.includes(oldId));
+            await Promise.all(changedUploads.map(async (upload) => {
+                upload.categories = remapCategoryList(upload.categories, oldId, newId);
+                await saveUpload(upload);
+            }));
+        }
+
+        async function removeCategoryFromAllItems(categoryId) {
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                setCategories(item, removeCategoryFromList(getCategories(item), categoryId));
+            });
+
+            const overrides = loadOverrides();
+            Object.keys(overrides).forEach((key) => {
+                overrides[key] = removeCategoryFromList(overrides[key], categoryId);
+            });
+            localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(overrides));
+
+            const hidden = loadHiddenRecords();
+            hidden.forEach((record) => {
+                record.categories = removeCategoryFromList(record.categories, categoryId);
+            });
+            saveHiddenRecords(hidden);
+
+            const uploads = await readUploads();
+            const changedUploads = uploads.filter((upload) => upload.categories?.includes(categoryId));
+            await Promise.all(changedUploads.map(async (upload) => {
+                upload.categories = removeCategoryFromList(upload.categories, categoryId);
+                await saveUpload(upload);
+            }));
+        }
+
+        function renderFilters() {
+            filtersNav.innerHTML = `
+                <button type="button" data-filter="all" aria-pressed="${activeFilter === "all" ? "true" : "false"}">All</button>
+                ${categories.map(({ id, label }) => `
+                    <button type="button" data-filter="${escapeAttr(id)}" aria-pressed="${activeFilter === id ? "true" : "false"}">${escapeAttr(label)}</button>
+                `).join("")}
+            `;
+        }
+
+        function renderCategoryList() {
+            categoryList.innerHTML = categories.map(({ id, label }) => `
+                <li class="category-list__item" data-category-id="${escapeAttr(id)}">
+                    <div class="category-list__fields">
+                        <label>
+                            Name
+                            <input type="text" class="category-list__label" value="${escapeAttr(label)}">
+                        </label>
+                        <p class="category-list__slug">Filter id: <code>${escapeAttr(id)}</code></p>
+                    </div>
+                    <div class="category-list__actions">
+                        <button type="button" data-action="save">Save</button>
+                        <button type="button" data-action="delete" class="category-menu__delete"${id === "other" ? " disabled" : ""}>Delete</button>
+                    </div>
+                </li>
+            `).join("");
+        }
+
+        async function renameCategory(categoryId, newLabel) {
+            const trimmed = newLabel.trim();
+            if (!trimmed) {
+                alert("Category name is required.");
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            const index = categories.findIndex((category) => category.id === categoryId);
+            if (index < 0) {
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            const newId = slugifyCategory(trimmed);
+            if (categories.some((category) => category.id === newId && category.id !== categoryId)) {
+                alert("A category with that name already exists.");
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            if (newId !== categoryId) {
+                migrateCategoryId(categoryId, newId);
+                await migrateCategoryIdInUploads(categoryId, newId);
+                categories[index].id = newId;
+                if (activeFilter === categoryId) {
+                    activeFilter = newId;
+                }
+            }
+
+            categories[index].label = trimmed;
+            saveCategoriesList();
+            renderFilters();
+            renderCategoryList();
+            applyFilter(activeFilter);
+            if (!hiddenArtworkPanel.hidden) {
+                await renderHiddenGallery();
+            }
+            return true;
+        }
+
+        async function addCategory(label) {
+            const trimmed = label.trim();
+            if (!trimmed) {
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            let id = slugifyCategory(trimmed);
+            if (categories.some((category) => category.id === id)) {
+                alert("A category with that name already exists.");
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            categories.push({ id, label: trimmed });
+            saveCategoriesList();
+            renderFilters();
+            renderCategoryList();
+            return true;
+        }
+
+        async function deleteCategory(categoryId) {
+            if (categoryId === "other") {
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            const usage = await countCategoryUsage(categoryId);
+            const label = getCategoryLabel(categoryId);
+            if (usage > 0) {

+            renderCategoryList();

I need more filters for what the image was created on. So inside digital I need iPad, Procreate, Solidworks, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Illustrator

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Exploring how categories and filtering work so we can add digital sub-filters for creation tools.

[REDACTED]

Implementing digital tool sub-filters: a secondary filter bar when Digital is selected, plus tagging and editing support.

[REDACTED]

Implementing the digital tool sub-filters and wiring them through tagging, filtering, and editing.

[REDACTED]

Digital artwork now has creation-tool sub-filters. Here's what was added:

**When you select Digital**, a second row of filters appears:
- All digital
- iPad
- Procreate
- Solidworks
- Adobe Photoshop
- Adobe Illustrator

**Existing digital images** were pre-tagged from their filenames (e.g. iPad/Procreate pieces got `ipad` and `procreate`).

**Right-click any image** → if Digital is checked, a **Created with** section lets you set which tools apply. That works for uploads, hidden artwork, and the add-artwork form too.

To tag Solidworks, Photoshop, or Illustrator pieces, right-click the image, ensure **Digital** is checked, then tick the relevant tool(s). Those choices are saved in this browser.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

             item.dataset.categories = unique.length ? unique.join(" ") : "other";

+        function getTools(item) {
+            return (item.dataset.tools || "").split(" ").filter(Boolean);
+        }
+
+        function setTools(item, tools) {
+            const unique = [...new Set(tools)].sort();
+            if (unique.length) {
+                item.dataset.tools = unique.join(" ");
+            } else {
+                delete item.dataset.tools;
+            }
+        }
+
+        function itemHasDigitalCategory(item) {
+            return getCategories(item).includes("digital");
+        }
+
+        function inferToolsFromText(text) {
+            const lower = String(text).toLowerCase();
+            const tools = [];
+            if (/\bipad\b/.test(lower)) {
+                tools.push("ipad");
+            }
+            if (/procreate/.test(lower)) {
+                tools.push("procreate");
+            }
+            if (/solid\s*works?/.test(lower)) {
+                tools.push("solidworks");
+            }
+            if (/photoshop/.test(lower)) {
+                tools.push("adobe-photoshop");
+            }
+            if (/illustrator/.test(lower)) {
+                tools.push("adobe-illustrator");
+            }
+            return [...new Set(tools)];
+        }
+
+        function inferToolsFromItem(item) {
+            const href = item.querySelector("a")?.getAttribute("href") || "";
+            const alt = item.querySelector("img")?.getAttribute("alt") || "";
+            return inferToolsFromText(`${href} ${alt}`);
+        }
+
         function filenameToAlt(filename) {
             return filename
                 .replace(/\.[^.]+$/, "")
@@ -690,6 +955,312 @@
                 .trim();
         }
 
+        function escapeAttr(value) {
+            return String(value)
+                .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
+                .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
+                .replace(/</g, "&lt;");
+        }
+
+        function slugifyCategory(label) {
+            const slug = label.toLowerCase().trim()
+                .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-")
+                .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, "");
+            return slug || "other";
+        }
+
+        function loadCategories() {
+            try {
+                const stored = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(CATEGORIES_STORAGE_KEY) || "null");
+                if (Array.isArray(stored) && stored.length) {
+                    return stored;
+                }
+            } catch {
+                // fall through to defaults
+            }
+            return DEFAULT_CATEGORIES.map((category) => ({ ...category }));
+        }
+
+        function saveCategoriesList() {
+            localStorage.setItem(CATEGORIES_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(categories));
+        }
+
+        function getCategoryLabel(categoryId) {
+            return categories.find((category) => category.id === categoryId)?.label || categoryId;
+        }
+
+        function remapCategoryList(list, oldId, newId) {
+            if (!Array.isArray(list) || !list.length) {
+                return ["other"];
+            }
+            const remapped = list.map((category) => (category === oldId ? newId : category));
+            const unique = [...new Set(remapped)];
+            return unique.length ? unique : ["other"];
+        }
+
+        function removeCategoryFromList(list, categoryId) {
+            if (!Array.isArray(list) || !list.length) {
+                return ["other"];
+            }
+            const filtered = list.filter((category) => category !== categoryId);
+            return filtered.length ? filtered : ["other"];
+        }
+
+        function mergeDiscoveredCategories() {
+            const known = new Set(categories.map((category) => category.id));
+            const discovered = [];
+
+            const registerCategory = (categoryId) => {
+                if (!known.has(categoryId)) {
+                    discovered.push(categoryId);
+                    known.add(categoryId);
+                }
+            };
+
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                getCategories(item).forEach(registerCategory);
+            });
+            loadHiddenRecords().forEach((record) => {
+                record.categories?.forEach(registerCategory);
+            });
+
+            discovered.sort().forEach((categoryId) => {
+                categories.push({
+                    id: categoryId,
+                    label: categoryId
+                        .split("-")
+                        .map((part) => part.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + part.slice(1))
+                        .join(" "),
+                });
+            });
+
+            if (discovered.length) {
+                saveCategoriesList();
+            }
+        }
+
+        async function countCategoryUsage(categoryId) {
+            let count = 0;
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                if (getCategories(item).includes(categoryId)) {
+                    count += 1;
+                }
+            });
+            loadHiddenRecords().forEach((record) => {
+                if (record.categories?.includes(categoryId)) {
+                    count += 1;
+                }
+            });
+            const uploads = await readUploads();
+            uploads.forEach((upload) => {
+                if (upload.categories?.includes(categoryId)) {
+                    count += 1;
+                }
+            });
+            return count;
+        }
+
+        function migrateCategoryId(oldId, newId) {
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                const itemCategories = getCategories(item);
+                if (itemCategories.includes(oldId)) {
+                    setCategories(item, remapCategoryList(itemCategories, oldId, newId));
+                }
+            });
+
+            const overrides = loadOverrides();
+            Object.keys(overrides).forEach((key) => {
+                if (overrides[key].includes(oldId)) {
+                    overrides[key] = remapCategoryList(overrides[key], oldId, newId);
+                }
+            });
+            localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(overrides));
+
+            const hidden = loadHiddenRecords();
+            hidden.forEach((record) => {
+                record.categories = remapCategoryList(record.categories, oldId, newId);
+            });
+            saveHiddenRecords(hidden);
+        }
+
+        async function migrateCategoryIdInUploads(oldId, newId) {
+            const uploads = await readUploads();
+            const changedUploads = uploads.filter((upload) => upload.categories?.includes(oldId));
+            await Promise.all(changedUploads.map(async (upload) => {
+                upload.categories = remapCategoryList(upload.categories, oldId, newId);
+                await saveUpload(upload);
+            }));
+        }
+
+        async function removeCategoryFromAllItems(categoryId) {
+            gallery.querySelectorAll("li").forEach((item) => {
+                setCategories(item, removeCategoryFromList(getCategories(item), categoryId));
+            });
+
+            const overrides = loadOverrides();
+            Object.keys(overrides).forEach((key) => {
+                overrides[key] = removeCategoryFromList(overrides[key], categoryId);
+            });
+            localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(overrides));
+
+            const hidden = loadHiddenRecords();
+            hidden.forEach((record) => {
+                record.categories = removeCategoryFromList(record.categories, categoryId);
+            });
+            saveHiddenRecords(hidden);
+
+            const uploads = await readUploads();
+            const changedUploads = uploads.filter((upload) => upload.categories?.includes(categoryId));
+            await Promise.all(changedUploads.map(async (upload) => {
+                upload.categories = removeCategoryFromList(upload.categories, categoryId);
+                await saveUpload(upload);
+            }));
+        }
+
+        function renderFilters() {
+            filtersNav.innerHTML = `
+                <button type="button" data-filter="all" aria-pressed="${activeFilter === "all" ? "true" : "false"}">All</button>
+                ${categories.map(({ id, label }) => `
+                    <button type="button" data-filter="${escapeAttr(id)}" aria-pressed="${activeFilter === id ? "true" : "false"}">${escapeAttr(label)}</button>
+                `).join("")}
+            `;
+            renderDigitalFilters();
+        }
+
+        function renderDigitalFilters() {
+            const showDigitalTools = activeFilter === "digital";
+            digitalFiltersNav.hidden = !showDigitalTools;
+            if (!showDigitalTools) {
+                digitalFiltersNav.innerHTML = "";
+                return;
+            }
+
+            digitalFiltersNav.innerHTML = `
+                <button type="button" data-digital-filter="all" aria-pressed="${activeDigitalTool === "all" ? "true" : "false"}">All digital</button>
+                ${DIGITAL_TOOLS.map(({ id, label }) => `
+                    <button type="button" data-digital-filter="${escapeAttr(id)}" aria-pressed="${activeDigitalTool === id ? "true" : "false"}">${escapeAttr(label)}</button>
+                `).join("")}
+            `;
+        }
+
+        function renderToolCheckboxes(selectedTools = []) {
+            const selected = new Set(selectedTools);
+            return DIGITAL_TOOLS.map(({ id, label }) => `
+                <label>
+                    <input type="checkbox" name="tool" value="${id}" ${selected.has(id) ? "checked" : ""}>
+                    ${label}
+                </label>
+            `).join("");
+        }
+
+        function renderCategoryList() {
+            categoryList.innerHTML = categories.map(({ id, label }) => `
+                <li class="category-list__item" data-category-id="${escapeAttr(id)}">
+                    <div class="category-list__fields">
+                        <label>
+                            Name
+                            <input type="text" class="category-list__label" value="${escapeAttr(label)}">
+                        </label>
+                        <p class="category-list__slug">Filter id: <code>${escapeAttr(id)}</code></p>
+                    </div>
+                    <div class="category-list__actions">
+                        <button type="button" data-action="save">Save</button>
+                        <button type="button" data-action="delete" class="category-menu__delete"${id === "other" ? " disabled" : ""}>Delete</button>
+                    </div>
+                </li>
+            `).join("");
+        }
+
+        async function renameCategory(categoryId, newLabel) {
+

GitHub Pages deployment is set up and live.

**Live site:** https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/

### What was done

1. **`.github/workflows/pages.yml`** — deploys the site automatically on every push to `main`
2. **`.nojekyll`** — tells GitHub Pages to serve the static files as-is (no Jekyll processing)
3. **`README.md`** — added deploy instructions
4. **Pushed to `main`** — the workflow ran successfully (Deploy to GitHub Pages #1)

### Going forward

After you change the gallery, deploy with:

```powershell
git add .
git commit -m "Your message"
git push origin main
```

Changes usually appear within 1–2 minutes.

### Optional check in GitHub

If you want to confirm the setup:

1. Open [repository Settings → Pages](https://github.com/waynebb7/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/settings/pages)
2. Under **Build and deployment**, **Source** can be **GitHub Actions** (recommended) or **Deploy from a branch** — both work; the new workflow uses GitHub Actions

Your portfolio is published. Open the live URL above to confirm everything looks right.

I need this to be private unless I give someone a pass code to gain access to the page. Plus I need to have a read only version for viewers and an admin version for me to be able to update it.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

This needs a viewer/admin split on a static GitHub Pages site. I'll inspect the current page structure and plan a passcode gate with read-only vs admin modes.

[REDACTED]

Implementing a passcode gate with viewer (read-only) and admin modes. On GitHub Pages this is client-side privacy—not bank-grade security, but it keeps casual visitors out.

[REDACTED]

Passcode-protected **viewer** and **admin** modes are in place. Here’s how it works:

### Viewer (read only)
- Open: **https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/**
- Default passcode: `gallery-view`
- Can browse, filter, and open full-size images
- Cannot add/edit/hide artwork, manage categories, or right-click to edit

### Admin (full editing)
- Open: **https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/admin.html**
- Default passcode: `wayne-admin`
- Unlocks all editing tools (add artwork, hidden artwork, manage categories, export, right-click menu)

### Before you share the site
Change the passcodes in `index.html` — search for `ACCESS_CONFIG`:

```javascript
const ACCESS_CONFIG = {
    viewer: { passcode: "gallery-view", ... },
    admin: { passcode: "wayne-admin", ... },
};
```

Replace those with your own passcodes, then commit and push to deploy.

### Important limitation
Because this runs on **GitHub Pages** (static hosting), the passcode check happens in the browser. That keeps casual visitors out, but it is **not** strong security — someone technical could inspect the page source. For stronger protection you’d need server-side auth (e.g. Cloudflare Access).

When you’re ready, I can commit and push these changes for you.

[REDACTED]

Where can I document the passcodes safely and securely

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

You should **not** store the real passcodes in anything that ships with the site or sits in the public repo.

### Do not put them here
- `index.html` (after you go live — anyone can read the source)
- `README.md`
- Git commits, issues, or PRs
- Email, chat, or shared docs unless encrypted and access-controlled

### Safe places to keep them

**1. Password manager (best option)**  
Use 1Password, Bitwarden, Apple Passwords, etc. Create two entries:

- `Art Gallery – Viewer`
- `Art Gallery – Admin`

Store the passcodes, the live URL, and `admin.html` URL there. Share the **viewer** entry only with people who should see the gallery.

**2. Private offline note**  
A notebook or encrypted file on your own machine is fine for your backup copy.

**3. GitHub repository secrets**  
Only useful if you later add server-side auth or a build step. They **do not** protect a plain static `index.html` on GitHub Pages.

### How this fits your current setup

Right now the passcodes live in `ACCESS_CONFIG` inside `index.html` because GitHub Pages has no server. That means:

- The passcode strings are visible to anyone who inspects the page source
- The gate stops casual visitors, but it is not strong security

**Practical approach:**

1. **Change** the default passcodes in `ACCESS_CONFIG` before sharing the site  
2. **Record** the real values only in your password manager  
3. **Share** only the viewer passcode with guests (via password manager share, or tell them in person)

### If you want better protection later

For real security you’d need something outside the static page, for example:

- Cloudflare Access in front of the site  
- A host with server-side login  
- A private repo + GitHub Pages on a paid plan (still not the same as passcode auth on the page itself)

**Bottom line:** document passcodes in a **password manager** or **private offline storage**. Treat `index.html` as the lock people can eventually study, not as the place to keep your secret list.

[REDACTED]

Implementing admin-only drag-and-drop reordering with cross-browser persistence via a committed `gallery-order.json`. Exploring the gallery structure and admin init flow.

[REDACTED]

Implementing admin-only drag-and-drop with `gallery-order.json` for cross-browser layout persistence.

[REDACTED]

Fixing gallery order load timing and removing a duplicate CSS rule.

[REDACTED]

Admin-only drag-and-drop reordering is in place, with layout shared across browsers via a published file.

### Admin: reorder tiles
1. Sign in at `admin.html`
2. Drag the **⋮⋮** handle on any tile
3. Drop it where you want in the gallery

### Publish layout (all browsers)
Because GitHub Pages is static, layout is stored in **`gallery-order.json`** in the repo:

1. After reordering, click **Export gallery layout**
2. Save `gallery-order.json` in the project root (next to `index.html`)
3. Commit and push to GitHub

After Pages redeploys (~1–2 minutes), viewers and admins see the same order everywhere.

### How it works
| Who | Drag handles | Layout source |
|-----|----------------|---------------|
| **Viewer** | Hidden | `gallery-order.json` only |
| **Admin** | Visible | Draft in browser until you export; then `gallery-order.json` |

A yellow notice appears in admin when you have unpublished layout changes.

### Note
Until you export and push `gallery-order.json`, reordering only affects your admin session. Other browsers keep the last published layout (or the default HTML order if no file exists yet).

When I drag the image tiles since there are 3 columns when I drag from the first column to the second it just moves the image to the end of the first column. This is no good, it needs topush the other tiles out of the way so that I can see exactly where I want to place it.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Drag-and-drop is updated for the 3-column masonry layout. Here’s what changed:

### What was wrong
The gallery uses **CSS columns**, which fill **top-to-bottom per column**, not left-to-right like a grid. The old logic only compared vertical position, so dragging from column 1 to column 2 often dropped the tile at the end of column 1 instead of where you were pointing.

### What’s fixed
1. **Column-aware positioning** — uses both mouse X (which column) and Y (where in that column) to find the drop slot.
2. **“Drop here” placeholder** — a dashed slot moves as you drag so you can see exactly where the tile will land.
3. **Tiles shift aside** — the dragged tile is hidden during the drag; the placeholder takes its place in the layout and pushes other tiles as it moves.
4. **DOM synced to visual order** — on drop, the gallery order is saved in column layout so it stays consistent.

Try dragging again in admin mode using the **⋮⋮** handle. You should see the placeholder move between columns and other tiles reflow around it.

After you’re happy with the layout, use **Export gallery layout** and commit `gallery-order.json` so viewers see the same order.

[REDACTED]

+        function getGalleryColumnCount() {
+            const width = gallery.getBoundingClientRect().width;
+            if (width <= 640) {
+                return 1;
+            }
+            if (width <= 900) {
+                return 2;
+            }
+            return 3;
+        }
+
+        function getItemColumnIndex(rect, galleryRect, columnCount) {
+            if (columnCount === 1) {
+                return 0;
+            }
+            const centerX = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - galleryRect.left;
+            const columnWidth = galleryRect.width / columnCount;
+            return Math.min(columnCount - 1, Math.max(0, Math.floor(centerX / columnWidth)));
+        }
+
+        function getVisualOrderedItems(excludeItem = null) {
+            const galleryRect = gallery.getBoundingClientRect();
+            const columnCount = getGalleryColumnCount();
+            const items = [...gallery.querySelectorAll("li")].filter((item) => {
+                if (item === excludeItem || item === dropPlaceholder || item.classList.contains("is-dragging")) {
+                    return false;
+                }
+                if (item.classList.contains("is-hidden")) {
+                    return false;
+                }
+                return true;
+            });
+
+            const buckets = Array.from({ length: columnCount }, () => []);
+            items.forEach((item) => {
+                const rect = item.getBoundingClientRect();
+                const columnIndex = item.classList.contains("gallery-item--wide")
+                    ? 0
+                    : getItemColumnIndex(rect, galleryRect, columnCount);
+                buckets[columnIndex].push({ item, top: rect.top, left: rect.left });
+            });
+
+            buckets.forEach((bucket) => {
+                bucket.sort((a, b) => a.top - b.top || a.left - b.left);
+            });
+
+            return buckets.flatMap((bucket) => bucket.map((entry) => entry.item));
+        }
+
+        function getInsertReference(clientX, clientY, draggedItem) {
+            const galleryRect = gallery.getBoundingClientRect();
+            const columnCount = getGalleryColumnCount();
+            const columnWidth = galleryRect.width / columnCount;
+            const pointerColumn = Math.min(
+                columnCount - 1,
+                Math.max(0, Math.floor((clientX - galleryRect.left) / columnWidth))
+            );
+
+            const ordered = getVisualOrderedItems(draggedItem);
+            const itemsByColumn = Array.from({ length: columnCount }, () => []);
+            ordered.forEach((item) => {
+                const rect = item.getBoundingClientRect();
+                const columnIndex = item.classList.contains("gallery-item--wide")
+                    ? 0
+                    : getItemColumnIndex(rect, galleryRect, columnCount);
+                itemsByColumn[columnIndex].push({ item, rect });
+            });
+
+            const columnItems = itemsByColumn[pointerColumn];
+            for (const { item, rect } of columnItems) {
+                if (clientY < rect.top + rect.height / 2) {
+                    return item;
+                }
+            }
+
+            if (columnItems.length) {
+                const lastItem = columnItems[columnItems.length - 1].item;
+                const lastIndex = ordered.indexOf(lastItem);
+                if (lastIndex + 1 < ordered.length) {
+                    return ordered[lastIndex + 1];
+                }
+                return null;
+            }
+
+            for (let columnIndex = pointerColumn + 1; columnIndex < columnCount; columnIndex += 1) {
+                if (itemsByColumn[columnIndex].length) {
+                    return itemsByColumn[columnIndex][0].item;
+                }
+            }
+
+            return null;
+        }
+
+        function ensureDropPlaceholder() {
+            if (dropPlaceholder) {
+                return dropPlaceholder;
+            }
+            dropPlaceholder = document.createElement("li");
+            dropPlaceholder.className = "gallery-drop-placeholder";
+            dropPlaceholder.setAttribute("aria-hidden", "true");
+            return dropPlaceholder;
+        }
+
+        function removeDropPlaceholder() {
+            if (dropPlaceholder?.parentElement) {
+                dropPlaceholder.remove();
+            }
+        }
+
+        function buildLayoutWithPlaceholder(insertBefore) {
+            const ordered = getVisualOrderedItems(draggedGalleryItem);
+            const placeholder = ensureDropPlaceholder();
+            const layout = [...ordered];
+
+            if (insertBefore) {
+                const index = layout.indexOf(insertBefore);
+                layout.splice(index >= 0 ? index : layout.length, 0, placeholder);
+            } else {
+                layout.push(placeholder);
+            }
+
+            return layout;
+        }
+
+        function applyGalleryLayoutOrder(layout) {
+            layout.forEach((element) => gallery.appendChild(element));
+            if (draggedGalleryItem?.parentElement === gallery) {
+                gallery.appendChild(draggedGalleryItem);

+        }
+

+                return;
+            }
+
+            const insertBefore = getInsertReference(clientX, clientY, draggedGalleryItem);

I'll inspect the drag-and-drop implementation and how the gallery layout interacts with it.

[REDACTED]

CSS multi-column layout rebalances tiles by height, so DOM reordering doesn't match visual columns. Switching to an explicit column grid and simplifying drag logic so placeholders land in the correct slot.

[REDACTED]

Handling wide tiles and preventing placeholder flicker during drag.

[REDACTED]

The root cause was **CSS multi-column layout**. The browser balances tile heights across columns automatically, so reordering the DOM never reliably matched where you were pointing — especially when moving from column 1 to column 2.

### What changed

1. **Gallery layout** — Switched from `column-count` masonry to a **3-column CSS grid** with `grid-auto-flow: column`. DOM order now maps directly to visual columns: items fill column 1 top-to-bottom, then column 2, then column 3.

2. **Simpler drag logic** — Removed the full-gallery rebuild on every `dragover`. The placeholder is inserted at the exact DOM position for your pointer column and Y position, and other tiles shift around it.

3. **Wide tiles** — Still span all columns via `grid-column: 1 / -1`, with drop targeting when you drag over them.

### What you should see

- Dragging from column 1 to column 2 moves the **“Drop here”** placeholder into column 2.
- Neighboring tiles reflow to show the exact drop slot.
- On drop, the tile lands where the placeholder was.

**Note:** The grid uses strict column ordering rather than height-balanced masonry, so the overall layout may look slightly different until you re-export and save `gallery-order.json`. Drag behavior should now be predictable.

Try it in admin mode with the **⋮⋮** handles and let me know if any specific drag case still misbehaves.

Also not working

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Investigating the drag implementation and testing a more reliable approach with explicit column containers.

[REDACTED]

CSS grid still fights drag placement. Switching to explicit column containers so each tile lives in a real column and drag targets are unambiguous.

[REDACTED]

Adding CSS so the flat HTML layout stays correct until JavaScript builds the column structure.

[REDACTED]

The drag logic was fighting the layout engine. CSS columns and grid both **recompute tile positions automatically**, so moving items in the DOM never reliably matched where you were pointing.

### Root fix: real column containers
The gallery now uses **three actual column lists** (flex layout):

- Each tile lives inside a specific column `<ul>`
- Dragging detects which **column** your pointer is over
- The **“Drop here”** placeholder is inserted only inside that column
- Other tiles in that column shift up/down around it
- On drop, the tile moves into that exact slot

### Other fixes
- **Drag tracking** listens on both the gallery and `document`, so drag events aren’t lost when moving between columns
- **Order save/load** reads and writes column-by-column (left → right, top → bottom)
- **Initial page load** still uses the old masonry layout until JavaScript runs, then migrates tiles into columns without a layout flash

### How to test
1. Sign in as admin
2. Drag using the **⋮⋮** handle
3. Move a tile from column 1 into column 2 — you should see the dashed placeholder move within column 2 and tiles shift aside
4. Drop to confirm it lands there

If anything still misbehaves, tell me the exact move (e.g. “top of column 1 to middle of column 2”) and I can tune it further. After reordering, use **Export gallery layout** so the new column order is saved.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

Tile dragging does not work at all so remove tile dragging functionality completely and put it back to just before we started down the tile dragging path.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Reverting all tile-dragging changes and restoring the pre-drag gallery. Searching the codebase for drag-related code to remove.

[REDACTED]

Restoring `index.html` and `README.md` from the commit before drag-and-drop was added.

[REDACTED]

All tile dragging functionality has been removed by restoring `index.html` and `README.md` to the state from commit `0b75cc2` — the version right before drag-and-drop was added.

### Removed
- Drag handles (⋮⋮) on tiles
- Drag-and-drop reordering logic
- Column structure migration (`gallery-column`, `data-columns-ready`, etc.)
- Drop placeholder UI
- **Export gallery layout** button
- Layout status banner
- `gallery-order.json` loading/saving and draft storage in `localStorage`
- README instructions for drag-to-reorder and layout export

### Restored
- Original **CSS column masonry** layout (`column-count: 3`)
- Simple flat `<ul id="gallery">` with artwork `<li>` items directly inside

### Still in place
- Passcode gate (viewer vs admin)
- Add artwork, categories, hidden artwork, filters
- GitHub Pages deployment

The changes are staged in git but not committed. Say if you want me to commit and push them.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 7fb706f..f73fa8d 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -41,7 +41,7 @@ Share the **viewer** passcode with people you want to see the gallery. Keep the
 ### How it works
 
 - **Viewer mode** shows the published gallery only. It does not load browser-only edits, uploads, or hidden images.
-- **Admin mode** unlocks all editing tools (add artwork, drag-to-reorder tiles, right-click categories, hidden artwork, manage categories, export).
+- **Admin mode** unlocks all editing tools (add artwork, right-click categories, hidden artwork, manage categories, export).
 - Use **Remember on this device** to stay signed in on that browser.
 - **Sign out** clears access and returns to the passcode screen.
 
@@ -119,24 +119,12 @@ Set `data-categories` to one or more of: `digital`, `manga`, `pumpkins`, `sketch
 
 You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
 
-## Reordering gallery tiles (admin)
-
-In **admin mode**, drag the **⋮⋮** handle on any tile to rearrange the gallery.
-
-1. Drag tiles into the order you want.
-2. Click **Export gallery layout** to download `gallery-order.json`.
-3. Save that file in the project root (next to `index.html`).
-4. Commit and push to GitHub.
-
-After GitHub Pages redeploys, every browser (including viewer mode) uses that layout. Until you export and publish the JSON file, layout changes are only stored as an admin draft in your browser.
-
 ## Project structure
 
 | Path | Purpose |
 | --- | --- |
 | `index.html` | Gallery page with passcode gate |
 | `admin.html` | Shortcut to admin sign in |
-| `gallery-order.json` | Published tile order (created when you export layout) |
 | `images/` | Full-resolution originals (used when an image is clicked) |
 | `images/display/` | Optimized display images (max 800px wide) |
 | `optimize_images.py` | Regenerates files in `images/display/` from `index.html` |

In the hidden artwork section when I delete an image I need it to actually delete the image completely from the folder structure and I also need it to ask me twice as a warning.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Exploring how hidden artwork deletion works and what the project structure supports.

[REDACTED]

Implementing double confirmation and permanent deletion: removing entries from the gallery, downloading an updated `index.html`, and providing scripts to delete image files from disk (static site can't delete server files directly from the browser).

[REDACTED]

Permanent deletion from **Hidden artwork** now uses a double confirmation and removes built-in images from the project when you apply the downloaded files.

### Double warning
**Delete permanently** now shows two confirmation dialogs:
1. What will be deleted (including file paths for built-in images)
2. A final “cannot be undone” warning

### Added artwork (browser uploads)
Deleted immediately from IndexedDB — no project files involved.

### Built-in gallery images
Because GitHub Pages is static, the browser cannot delete files on the server directly. When you confirm twice, the app:

1. Removes the item from hidden storage and the live gallery
2. Remembers the deletion so it does not reappear on reload
3. Downloads a deletion package:
   - **`index.html`** — gallery entry removed (when the page can fetch the current file)
   - **`delete-artwork.ps1`** — deletes files from `images/` and `images/display/`
   - **`gallery-deletion.json`** — for the Python script
   - **`gallery-deletion-steps.txt`** — instructions

### Apply the deletion to your project folder

```powershell
# Option A
.\delete-artwork.ps1

# Option B
python delete_artwork.py gallery-deletion.json
```

Then commit and push so the change is permanent on GitHub Pages.

A new **`delete_artwork.py`** script was added (similar to `optimize_images.py`), and the README documents this workflow.

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

         function getCategories(item) {

             localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(overrides));
         }
 
+        function clearToolOverride(imageKey) {
+            const overrides = loadToolOverrides();
+            delete overrides[imageKey];
+            localStorage.setItem(TOOLS_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(overrides));
+        }
+
         function loadHiddenRecords() {
             try {
                 const raw = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(HIDDEN_STORAGE_KEY) || "[]");
@@ -2026,6 +1728,128 @@
             });
         }
 
+        function loadPermanentDeletions() {
+            try {
+                const stored = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY) || "[]");
+                return Array.isArray(stored) ? stored : [];
+            } catch {
+                return [];
+            }
+        }
+
+        function savePermanentDeletions(records) {
+            localStorage.setItem(PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(records));
+        }
+
+        function addPermanentDeletion(record) {
+            const records = loadPermanentDeletions();
+            if (!records.some((entry) => entry.key === record.key)) {
+                records.push(record);
+                savePermanentDeletions(records);
+            }
+        }
+
+        function applyPermanentDeletions() {
+            const deletedHrefs = new Set(loadPermanentDeletions().map((entry) => entry.href));
+            gallery.querySelectorAll('li:not([data-uploaded="true"])').forEach((item) => {
+                const href = item.querySelector("a")?.getAttribute("href");
+                if (href && deletedHrefs.has(href)) {
+                    item.remove();
+                }
+            });
+        }
+
+        function confirmPermanentDeletion({ firstMessage, secondMessage }) {
+            if (!confirm(firstMessage)) {
+                return false;
+            }
+            return confirm(secondMessage);
+        }
+
+        function getBuiltinDeletionPaths(href, src) {
+            const files = [];
+            if (href?.startsWith("images/")) {
+                files.push(href);
+            }
+            if (src?.startsWith("images/") && !files.includes(src)) {
+                files.push(src);
+            }
+            return files;
+        }
+
+        function removeGalleryListItemFromHtml(html, href) {
+            const escaped = href.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&");
+            const pattern = new RegExp(
+                `\\n?[ \\t]*<li[^>]*>\\s*<a href="${escaped}"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?</a>\\s*</li>`,
+                "gi"
+            );
+            return html.replace(pattern, "");
+        }
+
+        async function fetchIndexHtml() {
+            try {
+                const response = await fetch("index.html", { cache: "no-store" });
+                if (!response.ok) {
+                    return null;
+                }
+                return await response.text();
+            } catch {
+                return null;
+            }
+        }
+
+        function buildDeletionPowerShell(files) {
+            const lines = [
+                "# Run from the project root (same folder as index.html).",
+                "$files = @(",
+                ...files.map((file) => `    "${file.replace(/\//g, "\\\\")}"`),
+                ")",
+                "foreach ($file in $files) {",
+                "    if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $file) {",
+                "        Remove-Item -LiteralPath $file -Force",
+                '        Write-Host "Deleted $file"',
+                "    } else {",
+                '        Write-Host "Not found: $file"',
+                "    }",
+                "}",
+            ];
+            return lines.join("\n");
+        }
+
+        async function exportBuiltinDeletionArtifacts() {
+            const deletions = loadPermanentDeletions();
+            const files = [...new Set(deletions.flatMap((entry) => getBuiltinDeletionPaths(entry.href, entry.src)))];
+            const html = await fetchIndexHtml();
+            const instructions = [
+                "Permanent artwork deletion",
+                "",
+                "1. Save the downloaded index.html over the project copy (if provided).",
+                "2. Run delete-artwork.ps1 from the project root, or run:",

  </git_diff_from_branch_to_main>
The add artwork function is not working. Is this because it is a static web page.

<span style="font-size:2em; color:green; font-weight:bold;">Assistant</span>

Investigating how add artwork works and what might be failing.

[REDACTED]

Checking for silent failures and improving error handling. Add artwork should work on static pages via browser storage, but only in admin mode.

[REDACTED]

Improving add-artwork reliability: it should work on static pages (browser storage), but errors were silent and new items could be hidden by active filters.

[REDACTED]

Partially yes — but **Add artwork can work** on a static page. It does not upload to the server. It saves images in your browser using **IndexedDB**, so they only exist locally until you export and publish them.

### Why it can seem broken

1. **Viewer mode** — Add artwork is admin-only. If you signed in with the viewer passcode, the button is hidden and uploads are disabled.
2. **Static site limits** — New images are **not** written to `images/` or `index.html` automatically. They stay in your browser until you use **Export added artwork** and commit the files to GitHub.
3. **Other visitors** — Uploaded artwork is hidden from viewer mode by design until you publish it.
4. **Active filters** — If a category filter was selected, newly added items could be added but hidden.
5. **Browser storage** — Opening the site as a local `file://` page or with storage blocked can prevent IndexedDB from working. Errors were previously silent.

### What I changed

- Clearer hint text explaining admin-only + browser-only storage
- Visible success/error messages in the Add artwork panel
- Storage check when opening Add artwork (with a helpful error if IndexedDB fails)
- After adding, the gallery switches to **All** so new items are visible
- Success message reminding you to use **Export added artwork**

### How to use it

1. Sign in with the **admin** passcode (`wayne-admin` by default, unless you changed it)
2. Open the site over **HTTPS** (e.g. https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/)
3. **Add artwork** → choose files → **Add to gallery**
4. You should see items at the top with a dashed purple outline
5. When ready to publish: **Export added artwork** → copy files into `images/` → update `index.html` → run `python optimize_images.py` → commit and push

If it still fails, tell me what message appears in the Add artwork panel (or whether you’re in admin vs viewer mode).

[REDACTED]

<span style="font-size:2em; color:blue; font-weight:bold;">User</span>

             });
         }
 
+        function loadPermanentDeletions() {
+            try {
+                const stored = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY) || "[]");
+                return Array.isArray(stored) ? stored : [];
+            } catch {
+                return [];
+            }
+        }
+
+        function savePermanentDeletions(records) {
+            localStorage.setItem(PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(records));
+        }
+
+        function addPermanentDeletion(record) {
+            const records = loadPermanentDeletions();
+            if (!records.some((entry) => entry.key === record.key)) {
+                records.push(record);
+                savePermanentDeletions(records);
+            }
+        }
+
+        function applyPermanentDeletions() {
+            const deletedHrefs = new Set(loadPermanentDeletions().map((entry) => entry.href));
+            gallery.querySelectorAll('li:not([data-uploaded="true"])').forEach((item) => {
+                const href = item.querySelector("a")?.getAttribute("href");
+                if (href && deletedHrefs.has(href)) {
+                    item.remove();
+                }
+            });
+        }
+
+        function confirmPermanentDeletion({ firstMessage, secondMessage }) {
+            if (!confirm(firstMessage)) {
+                return false;
+            }
+            return confirm(secondMessage);
+        }
+
+        function getBuiltinDeletionPaths(href, src) {
+            const files = [];
+            if (href?.startsWith("images/")) {
+                files.push(href);
+            }
+            if (src?.startsWith("images/") && !files.includes(src)) {
+                files.push(src);
+            }
+            return files;
+        }
+
+        function removeGalleryListItemFromHtml(html, href) {
+            const escaped = href.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&");
+            const pattern = new RegExp(
+                `\\n?[ \\t]*<li[^>]*>\\s*<a href="${escaped}"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?</a>\\s*</li>`,
+                "gi"
+            );
+            return html.replace(pattern, "");
+        }
+
+        async function fetchIndexHtml() {
+            try {
+                const response = await fetch("index.html", { cache: "no-store" });
+                if (!response.ok) {
+                    return null;
+                }
+                return await response.text();
+            } catch {
+                return null;
+            }
+        }
+
+        function buildDeletionPowerShell(files) {
+            const lines = [
+                "# Run from the project root (same folder as index.html).",
+                "$files = @(",
+                ...files.map((file) => `    "${file.replace(/\//g, "\\\\")}"`),
+                ")",
+                "foreach ($file in $files) {",
+                "    if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $file) {",
+                "        Remove-Item -LiteralPath $file -Force",
+                '        Write-Host "Deleted $file"',
+                "    } else {",
+                '        Write-Host "Not found: $file"',
+                "    }",
+                "}",
+            ];
+            return lines.join("\n");
+        }
+
+        async function exportBuiltinDeletionArtifacts() {
+            const deletions = loadPermanentDeletions();
+            const files = [...new Set(deletions.flatMap((entry) => getBuiltinDeletionPaths(entry.href, entry.src)))];
+            const html = await fetchIndexHtml();
+            const instructions = [
+                "Permanent artwork deletion",
+                "",
+                "1. Save the downloaded index.html over the project copy (if provided).",
+                "2. Run delete-artwork.ps1 from the project root, or run:",
+                "   python delete_artwork.py gallery-deletion.json",
+                "3. Commit and push the changes to GitHub.",
+                "",
+                "Deleted artwork:",
+                ...deletions.map((entry) => `- ${entry.alt} (${entry.href})`),
+                "",
+                "Files to remove:",
+                ...files.map((file) => `- ${file}`),
+            ];
+
+            downloadBlob(
+                new Blob([JSON.stringify({ deletions }, null, 2)], { type: "application/json" }),
+                "gallery-deletion.json"
+            );
+            downloadBlob(new Blob([buildDeletionPowerShell(files)], { type: "text/plain" }), "delete-artwork.ps1");
+            if (html) {
+                let updatedHtml = html;
+                deletions.forEach((entry) => {
+                    updatedHtml = removeGalleryListItemFromHtml(updatedHtml, entry.href);
+                });
+                downloadBlob(new Blob([updatedHtml], { type: "text/html" }), "index.html");
+            }
+            downloadBlob(new Blob([instructions.join("\n")], { type: "text/plain" }), "gallery-deletion-steps.txt");
+        }
+

diff --git a/CURSOR_CONVERSATION.md b/CURSOR_CONVERSATION.md

@@ -0,0 +1,247 @@
+# Cursor Conversation Context — Wayne Barrass-Brown Art Gallery
+
+**Purpose:** Load this file at the start of a new Cursor chat to restore project context from prior sessions.
+
+**Repository:** `d:\git\wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery`  
+**Live site:** https://waynebb7.github.io/wayne_barrass-brown_art_gallery/  
+**Transcript reference:** [f8a3e0ac-24c0-4150-bea5-2e55f6a851f3](f8a3e0ac-24c0-4150-bea5-2e55f6a851f3)
+
+---
+
+## Project summary
+
+Static single-page art portfolio for **Wayne Barrass-Brown**. Almost everything lives in `index.html` (~3000 lines) with CSS and JavaScript inline. Images are in `images/` (full resolution) and `images/display/` (optimized, max 800px wide via `optimize_images.py`).
+
+**Architecture:** GitHub Pages static site — no backend. Browser-only storage (localStorage, IndexedDB) for admin edits until exported and committed to git.
+
+---
+
+## Chronology of user requests and outcomes
+
+### Phase 1 — Gallery improvements (numbered list from early session)
+
+| # | Request | Done |
+|---|---------|------|
+| 1 | Fix broken HTML in header | Fixed mismatched `<h3>` tags |
+| 2 | Enable mobile viewing | Uncommented viewport meta tag |
+| 3 | Add meaningful alt text | Generated from filenames for all images |
+| 4 | Remove duplicate entries | Deduped gallery list items |
+| 5 | Load Poppins font | Added Google Fonts links |
+| 6 | Click to view full size | Wrapped images in `<a href="images/...">` |
+| 7 | Single layout, vertical scroll | Removed `page2.html`; `index.html` only, vertical column |
+| 8 | Lazy-load images | Added `loading="lazy"` |
+| 9 | Optimize image files | Added `optimize_images.py`, `images/display/` |
+| 10 | Use srcset / thumbnails | Display images served from `images/display/` |
+| 11 | Stop duplicating image list | Single `index.html` (page2 deleted) |
+| 12 | Clean up repo | `.gitignore`, removed `.DS_Store`, PSDs, unused images |
+| 13 | Basic metadata | Title, description, Open Graph |
+| 14 | Light categorization | Category filters + sub-filters (digital tools, sketch mediums) |
+| 15 | Deploy GitHub Pages | `.github/workflows/pages.yml`, `.nojekyll`, README deploy section |
+| 16 | Improve README | Full documentation |
+
+**Bug reports handled:**
+- `Rocks.jpg` / `page2.html` inconsistency → **Not applicable**; `page2.html` was intentionally deleted in step 7.
+- Missing link to `page2.html` → **Intentional**; alternate layout removed.
+
+### Phase 2 — Add artwork & admin features
+
+- **Add artwork panel** — pick images, set alt/categories, save to IndexedDB, show in gallery with dashed outline.
+- **Export added artwork** — downloads image files + `gallery-additions.txt` with HTML snippets.
+- **Right-click category menu** — edit categories/tools on any image.
+- **Hidden artwork** — hide from gallery (browser storage); restore or delete permanently.
+- **Manage categories** — add/rename/delete custom categories.
+- **Wide aspect tiles** — `gallery-item--wide` via `column-span: all` for panoramic images.
+
+### Phase 3 — Private access (viewer vs admin)
+
+- **Passcode gate** blocks page until sign-in.
+- **Viewer mode** — browse/filter/open images only; no admin UI; no browser-only edits/uploads.
+- **Admin mode** — full editing (add, hide, categories, export).
+- **`admin.html`** — shortcut that sets admin sign-in intent.
+- Default passcodes in `ACCESS_CONFIG` (change before sharing):
+  - Viewer: `gallery-view`
+  - Admin: `wayne-admin`
+- Passcodes verified via SHA-256 at runtime (`hashPasscode`, `verifyPasscode`).
+- **Security:** Client-side only — casual privacy, not strong auth. Store real passcodes in a password manager, not in README/repo.
+
+### Phase 4 — Tile drag-and-drop (later removed)
+
+- Implemented admin drag-to-reorder with `gallery-order.json` publish path.
+- Multiple attempts to fix cross-column masonry drag (CSS columns, grid, explicit column containers).
+- **User request:** Remove tile dragging completely → reverted to commit `0b75cc2` state (before drag was added).
+- **Current status:** No drag handles, no `gallery-order.json`, no layout export button.
+
+### Phase 5 — Permanent deletion from Hidden artwork
+
+- **Double confirmation** before permanent delete.
+- **Uploaded artwork** — deleted from IndexedDB immediately.
+- **Built-in gallery images** — browser cannot delete server files directly; downloads:
+  - `index.html` (with `<li>` removed, when fetch works)
+  - `delete-artwork.ps1`
+  - `gallery-deletion.json`
+  - `gallery-deletion-steps.txt`
+- **`delete_artwork.py`** — applies JSON deletion to `index.html` and deletes image files locally.
+- **`PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY`** — `gallery-permanent-deletions` in localStorage prevents deleted items reappearing until `index.html` is updated in repo.
+
+### Phase 6 — Add artwork troubleshooting
+
+- User reported add artwork not working; asked if static site was the cause.
+- **Answer:** Works in admin mode via IndexedDB, but only in that browser until exported. Not written to server automatically.
+- **Fixes added:** Status messages, IndexedDB probe, filter reset to "All" after add, clearer hints, admin-only guards.
+
+---
+
+## Current feature state (as of last session)
+
+### Working / in place
+
+- GitHub Pages deployment on push to `main`
+- Passcode gate (viewer + admin)
+- Category and sub-category filters
+- CSS column masonry layout (`column-count: 3`, responsive 2/1)
+- Add artwork (admin, IndexedDB)
+- Export added artwork
+- Hidden artwork (hide / restore / permanent delete with export workflow)
+- Manage categories
+- Right-click category menu (admin)
+- `optimize_images.py` and `delete_artwork.py`
+- README with deploy, access, add/delete workflows
+
+### Removed / not present
+
+- Tile drag-and-drop reordering
+- `gallery-order.json` layout publishing
+- `page2.html` alternate grid layout
+- Export gallery layout button
+
+### Uncommitted local changes (may differ from `main`)
+
+Recent work (drag removal, deletion workflow, add-artwork fixes) may be staged or uncommitted. Check `git status` and diff against `main` before assuming production matches local.
+
+---
+
+## Key files
+
+| Path | Purpose |
+|------|---------|
+| `index.html` | Entire gallery app (HTML, CSS, JS, passcodes as hashes) |
+| `admin.html` | Admin sign-in shortcut |
+| `README.md` | User-facing documentation |
+| `optimize_images.py` | Regenerate `images/display/` from `index.html` |
+| `delete_artwork.py` | Remove entries from `index.html` + delete image files |
+| `.github/workflows/pages.yml` | GitHub Pages deploy |
+| `.nojekyll` | Disable Jekyll on GitHub Pages |
+| `images/` | Full-resolution originals |
+| `images/display/` | Web-optimized display images |
+
+---
+
+## JavaScript constants & storage keys
+
+```text
+ACCESS_CONFIG              — passcode labels + SHA-256 hashes
+ACCESS_STORAGE_KEY         — remembered access role
+ACCESS_INTENT_KEY          — admin.html redirect intent
+STORAGE_KEY                  — gallery-category-overrides (localStorage)
+TOOLS_STORAGE_KEY          — gallery-tool-overrides
+CATEGORIES_STORAGE_KEY       — gallery-categories
+HIDDEN_STORAGE_KEY           — gallery-hidden-items
+PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY — gallery-permanent-deletions
+UPLOAD_DB_NAME               — art-gallery-uploads (IndexedDB)
+UPLOAD_STORE_NAME            — uploads
+WIDE_ASPECT_RATIO            — 1.55 (wide tile threshold)
+```
+
+**Default categories:** digital, manga, pumpkins, sketches, figures, painting, photos, other
+
+**Sub-filters:** digital (iPad, Procreate, Solidworks, Photoshop, Illustrator); sketches (pen, pencil, coloured pencil, charcoal)
+
+---
+
+## Admin workflows
+
+### Add new artwork (browser → published)
+
+1. Sign in as **admin**
+2. Add artwork → choose files → Add to gallery
+3. Export added artwork
+4. Copy images to `images/`
+5. Paste HTML into `<ul id="gallery">` in `index.html`
+6. `python optimize_images.py`
+7. Commit and push
+
+### Hide artwork
+
+- Right-click → Hide from gallery
+- View in **Hidden artwork** panel
+- **Show in gallery** to restore
+
+### Permanently delete hidden artwork
+
+1. Hidden artwork → Delete permanently (two confirmations)
+2. For built-in images: apply downloaded `index.html`, run `delete-artwork.ps1` or `python delete_artwork.py gallery-deletion.json`
+3. Commit and push
+
+### Change passcodes
+
+Edit `ACCESS_CONFIG` in `index.html` — use SHA-256 hashes, not plain text in production.
+
+---
+
+## Git commit history (relevant)
+
+```text
+bedfa74 — Add drag-and-drop (later reverted locally)
+0b75cc2 — Implement access gate (viewer/admin)  ← state before drag
+3581232 — Add GitHub Pages deployment workflow
+73cd56c — Enhanced index.html (filters, styling, hidden artwork UI)
+```
+
+---
+
+## Known limitations
+
+1. **Static site** — browser cannot write to `images/` or `index.html` on the server; export + git push required.
+2. **Passcodes** — visible to anyone who inspects `index.html`; hashes are not secret.
+3. **Admin edits** — category overrides, hidden items, uploads persist per-browser unless exported/published.
+4. **Viewer mode** — does not see browser-added uploads or hidden-item state.
+5. **Add artwork** — needs IndexedDB + HTTPS (GitHub Pages or local server); `file://` may fail.
+6. **Tile reordering** — removed by user request; do not re-implement without explicit ask.
+
+---
+
+## User preferences observed
+
+- Wants **minimal scope** changes; dislikes over-engineering.
+- **Removed** tile drag-and-drop after multiple failed UX attempts — do not restore unless asked.
+- Wants **double confirmation** for destructive deletes.
+- Wants **real file deletion** from project folder (via scripts, not browser-only hide).
+- Uses **Windows / PowerShell**.
+- **Do not commit** unless explicitly asked.
+- Store passcodes in **password manager**, not in repo/README.
+
+---
+
+## Suggested prompt for next session
+
+Copy into a new Cursor chat:
+
+```text
+Read CURSOR_CONVERSATION.md and README.md for context on this art gallery project.
+Continue from the current state described there.
+```
+
+Or attach `@CURSOR_CONVERSATION.md` when starting a chat.
+
+---
+
+## Open questions / possible follow-ups
+
+- [ ] Commit and push recent changes (drag removal, deletion workflow, add-artwork fixes) if not yet on `main`
+- [ ] Change default passcodes in `ACCESS_CONFIG` before wider sharing
+- [ ] User may want stronger auth later (Cloudflare Access, etc.) — not implemented
+- [ ] Tile reordering was explicitly abandoned — alternative: manual HTML order or future different UX
+

 You can also right-click any image and change its categories. Those edits are saved in localStorage for that browser only.
 
+## Permanently deleting hidden artwork (admin)
+
+In **Hidden artwork**, **Delete permanently** asks for confirmation twice.
+
+- **Added artwork** (browser uploads) is erased from this browser immediately.
+- **Built-in gallery images** are removed from the gallery and the browser records a permanent deletion. Your browser downloads:
+  - `index.html` with the gallery entry removed (when the page can fetch the current file)
+  - `delete-artwork.ps1` to delete the original and display image files
+  - `gallery-deletion.json` for the Python alternative below
+  - `gallery-deletion-steps.txt` with instructions
+
+Apply the deletion to the project folder:
+
+```powershell
+# Option A: PowerShell
+.\delete-artwork.ps1
+
+# Option B: Python
+python delete_artwork.py gallery-deletion.json
+```
+
+Then commit and push so GitHub Pages stops serving the removed images.
+

diff --git a/__pycache__/delete_artwork.cpython-314.pyc b/__pycache__/delete_artwork.cpython-314.pyc
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..eb936ee
Binary files /dev/null and b/__pycache__/delete_artwork.cpython-314.pyc differ
diff --git a/delete_artwork.py b/delete_artwork.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..6e78c9f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/delete_artwork.py
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+"""Remove artwork entries from index.html and delete image files."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import json
+import re
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+ROOT = Path(__file__).parent
+INDEX = ROOT / "index.html"
+
+
+def remove_gallery_item(html: str, href: str) -> str:
+    escaped = re.escape(href)
+    pattern = rf"\s*<li[^>]*>\s*<a href=\"{escaped}\"[^>]*>.*?</a>\s*</li>"
+    return re.sub(pattern, "", html, count=1, flags=re.DOTALL)
+
+
+def deletion_paths(href: str, src: str | None) -> list[Path]:
+    paths: list[Path] = []
+    if href.startswith("images/"):
+        paths.append(ROOT / href)
+    if src and src.startswith("images/") and ROOT / src not in paths:
+        paths.append(ROOT / src)
+    return paths
+
+
+def delete_files(paths: list[Path]) -> None:
+    for path in paths:
+        if path.exists():
+            path.unlink()
+            print(f"Deleted {path.relative_to(ROOT)}")
+        else:
+            print(f"Not found: {path.relative_to(ROOT)}")
+
+
+def apply_deletions(deletions: list[dict[str, str]]) -> None:
+    if not INDEX.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Missing {INDEX}")
+
+    html = INDEX.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    all_paths: list[Path] = []
+
+    for entry in deletions:
+        href = entry.get("href", "")
+        src = entry.get("src", "")
+        if not href:
+            continue
+        html = remove_gallery_item(html, href)
+        all_paths.extend(deletion_paths(href, src))
+
+    INDEX.write_text(html, encoding="utf-8")
+    print(f"Updated {INDEX.name}")
+
+    for path in dict.fromkeys(all_paths):
+        delete_files([path])
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
+        raise SystemExit("Usage: python delete_artwork.py gallery-deletion.json")
+
+    payload_path = Path(sys.argv[1])
+    payload = json.loads(payload_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    deletions = payload.get("deletions", [])
+    if not deletions:
+        raise SystemExit("No deletions found in JSON file.")
+
+    apply_deletions(deletions)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

@@ -1721,6 +1754,128 @@
             });
         }
 
+        function loadPermanentDeletions() {
+            try {
+                const stored = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY) || "[]");
+                return Array.isArray(stored) ? stored : [];
+            } catch {
+                return [];
+            }
+        }
+
+        function savePermanentDeletions(records) {
+            localStorage.setItem(PERMANENT_DELETIONS_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(records));
+        }
+
+        function addPermanentDeletion(record) {
+            const records = loadPermanentDeletions();
+            if (!records.some((entry) => entry.key === record.key)) {
+                records.push(record);
+                savePermanentDeletions(records);
+            }
+        }
+
+        function applyPermanentDeletions() {
+            const deletedHrefs = new Set(loadPermanentDeletions().map((entry) => entry.href));
+            gallery.querySelectorAll('li:not([data-uploaded="true"])').forEach((item) => {
+                const href = item.querySelector("a")?.getAttribute("href");
+                if (href && deletedHrefs.has(href)) {
+                    item.remove();
+                }
+            });
+        }
+
+        function confirmPermanentDeletion({ firstMessage, secondMessage }) {
+            if (!confirm(firstMessage)) {
+                return false;
+            }
+            return confirm(secondMessage);
+        }
+
+        function getBuiltinDeletionPaths(href, src) {
+            const files = [];
+            if (href?.startsWith("images/")) {
+                files.push(href);
+            }
+            if (src?.startsWith("images/") && !files.includes(src)) {
+                files.push(src);
+            }
+            return files;
+        }
+
+        function removeGalleryListItemFromHtml(html, href) {
+            const escaped = href.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&");
+            const pattern = new RegExp(
+                `\\n?[ \\t]*<li[^>]*>\\s*<a href="${escaped}"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?</a>\\s*</li>`,
+                "gi"
+            );
+            return html.replace(pattern, "");
+        }
+
+        async function fetchIndexHtml() {
+            try {
+                const response = await fetch("index.html", { cache: "no-store" });
+                if (!response.ok) {
+                    return null;
+                }
+                return await response.text();
+            } catch {
+                return null;
+            }
+        }
+
+        function buildDeletionPowerShell(files) {
+            const lines = [
+                "# Run from the project root (same folder as index.html).",
+                "$files = @(",
+                ...files.map((file) => `    "${file.replace(/\//g, "\\\\")}"`),
+                ")",
+                "foreach ($file in $files) {",
+                "    if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $file) {",
+                "        Remove-Item -LiteralPath $file -Force",
+                '        Write-Host "Deleted $file"',
+                "    } else {",
+                '        Write-Host "Not found: $file"',
+                "    }",
+                "}",
+            ];
+            return lines.join("\n");
+        }
+
+        async function exportBuiltinDeletionArtifacts() {
+            const deletions = loadPermanentDeletions();
+            const files = [...new Set(deletions.flatMap((entry) => getBuiltinDeletionPaths(entry.href, entry.src)))];
+            const html = await fetchIndexHtml();
+            const instructions = [
+                "Permanent artwork deletion",
+                "",
+                "1. Save the downloaded index.html over the project copy (if provided).",
+                "2. Run delete-artwork.ps1 from the project root, or run:",
+                "   python delete_artwork.py gallery-deletion.json",
+                "3. Commit and push the changes to GitHub.",
+                "",
+                "Deleted artwork:",
+                ...deletions.map((entry) => `- ${entry.alt} (${entry.href})`),
+                "",
+                "Files to remove:",
+                ...files.map((file) => `- ${file}`),
+            ];
+
+            downloadBlob(
+                new Blob([JSON.stringify({ deletions }, null, 2)], { type: "application/json" }),
+                "gallery-deletion.json"
+            );
+            downloadBlob(new Blob([buildDeletionPowerShell(files)], { type: "text/plain" }), "delete-artwork.ps1");
+            if (html) {
+                let updatedHtml = html;
+                deletions.forEach((entry) => {
+                    updatedHtml = removeGalleryListItemFromHtml(updatedHtml, entry.href);
+                });
+                downloadBlob(new Blob([updatedHtml], { type: "text/html" }), "index.html");
+            }
+            downloadBlob(new Blob([instructions.join("\n")], { type: "text/plain" }), "gallery-deletion-steps.txt");
+        }
+
         function getVisibleUploads(uploads) {
             return uploads.filter((upload) => !upload.hidden);
         }

diff --git a/CURSOR_CONVERSATION.md b/CURSOR_CONVERSATION.md
